1→defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListProjects do
     2→  @moduledoc """
     3→  List all projects accessible to the authenticated user.
     4→  """
     5→
     6→  use Hermes.Server.Component, type: :tool
     7→
     8→  alias Hermes.Server.Response
     9→  alias Tuist.MCP.Authorization
    10→  alias Tuist.Projects
    11→
    12→  schema do
    13→  end
    14→
    15→  @impl true
    16→  def execute(_arguments, frame) do
    17→    subject = Authorization.authenticated_subject(frame.assigns)
    18→    projects = Projects.list_accessible_projects(subject, preload: [:account])
    19→
    20→    data =
    21→      Enum.map(projects, fn project ->
    22→        %{
    23→          id: project.id,
    24→          name: project.name,
    25→          account_handle: project.account.name,
    26→          full_handle: "#{project.account.name}/#{project.name}"
    27→        }
    28→      end)
    29→
    30→    {:reply, Response.json(Response.tool(), data), frame}
    31→  end
    32→end
    33→

1→defmodule Tuist.MCP.Authorization do
     2→  @moduledoc false
     3→
     4→  alias Tuist.Authorization
     5→
     6→  def authorize(subject, action, resource, category) do
     7→    Authorization.authorize(:"#{category}_#{action}", subject, resource) == :ok
     8→  end
     9→
    10→  def authenticated_subject(assigns) when is_map(assigns) do
    11→    assigns[:current_subject] || assigns[:current_user] || assigns[:current_project]
    12→  end
    13→end
    14→

1→defmodule Tuist.MCP.Formatter do
     2→  @moduledoc false
     3→
     4→  def iso8601(value), do: iso8601(value, naive: :naive)
     5→
     6→  def iso8601(nil, _opts), do: nil
     7→  def iso8601(%DateTime{} = dt, _opts), do: DateTime.to_iso8601(dt)
     8→
     9→  def iso8601(%NaiveDateTime{} = dt, opts) do
    10→    case Keyword.get(opts, :naive, :naive) do
    11→      :utc ->
    12→        dt
    13→        |> NaiveDateTime.truncate(:second)
    14→        |> DateTime.from_naive!("Etc/UTC")
    15→        |> DateTime.to_iso8601()
    16→
    17→      _ ->
    18→        dt
    19→        |> NaiveDateTime.truncate(:second)
    20→        |> NaiveDateTime.to_iso8601()
    21→    end
    22→  end
    23→
    24→  def iso8601(other, _opts), do: to_string(other)
    25→end
    26→

1→defmodule TuistWeb.RateLimit.MCP do
     2→  @moduledoc """
     3→  Rate limiting for MCP endpoints.
     4→  """
     5→
     6→  alias Tuist.Accounts.AuthenticatedAccount
     7→  alias Tuist.Accounts.User
     8→  alias Tuist.Projects.Project
     9→  alias TuistWeb.Authentication
    10→  alias TuistWeb.RateLimit.InMemory
    11→
    12→  def hit(conn) do
    13→    {key, bucket_size} = key_and_bucket_size(conn)
    14→    InMemory.hit(key, to_timeout(minute: 1), bucket_size)
    15→  end
    16→
    17→  defp key_and_bucket_size(conn) do
    18→    case Authentication.authenticated_subject(conn) do
    19→      nil ->
    20→        {"mcp:unauth:#{TuistWeb.RemoteIp.get(conn)}", Tuist.Environment.mcp_rate_limit_bucket_size()}
    21→
    22→      %User{id: id} ->
    23→        {"mcp:auth:user:#{id}", Tuist.Environment.mcp_rate_limit_bucket_size()}
    24→
    25→      %Project{id: id} ->
    26→        {"mcp:auth:project:#{id}", Tuist.Environment.mcp_rate_limit_bucket_size()}
    27→
    28→      %AuthenticatedAccount{account: %{id: id}} ->
    29→        {"mcp:auth:account:#{id}", Tuist.Environment.mcp_rate_limit_bucket_size()}
    30→    end
    31→  end
    32→end
    33→

1→defmodule TuistWeb.Plugs.MCPRateLimitPlug do
     2→  @moduledoc false
     3→
     4→  use TuistWeb, :controller
     5→
     6→  alias TuistWeb.RateLimit
     7→
     8→  def init(opts), do: opts
     9→
    10→  def call(%Plug.Conn{} = conn, _opts) do
    11→    case RateLimit.MCP.hit(conn) do
    12→      {:allow, _} ->
    13→        conn
    14→
    15→      {:deny, _} ->
    16→        conn
    17→        |> put_status(:too_many_requests)
    18→        |> json(%{
    19→          jsonrpc: "2.0",
    20→          id: Map.get(conn.params, "id"),
    21→          error: %{code: -32_603, message: "Rate limit exceeded. Please try again later."}
    22→        })
    23→        |> halt()
    24→    end
    25→  end
    26→end
    27→

1→defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListTestCases do
     2→  @moduledoc """

     4→  """
     5→
     6→  use Hermes.Server.Component, type: :tool
     7→
     8→  alias Hermes.Server.Response
     9→  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.ToolSupport
    10→  alias Tuist.MCP.Formatter

    16→  schema do

    18→      required: true,
    19→      description: "The account handle (organization or user)."
    20→
    21→    field :project_handle, :string,
    22→      required: true,
    23→      description: "The project handle."
    24→
    25→    field :flaky, :boolean, description: "When true, returns only flaky test cases."
    26→    field :quarantined, :boolean, description: "Filter by quarantined status."
    27→    field :module_name, :string, description: "Filter by module name."
    28→    field :name, :string, description: "Filter by test case name."
    29→    field :suite_name, :string, description: "Filter by suite name."
    30→    field :page, :integer, description: "Page number (default: 1)."

    38→             account_handle,

    41→             @authorization_action,
    42→             @authorization_category,
    43→             "You do not have access to project: #{account_handle}/#{project_handle}"
    44→           ) do
    45→      page = arguments |> Map.get(:page) |> integer_argument(1)
    46→      page_size = arguments |> Map.get(:page_size) |> integer_argument(20) |> min(100)
    47→      filters = build_filters(arguments)
    48→
    49→      {test_cases, meta} =
    50→        Tests.list_test_cases(project.id, %{
    51→          filters: filters,
    52→          order_by: [:last_ran_at],
    53→          order_directions: [:desc],
    54→          page: page,
    55→          page_size: page_size
    56→        })
    57→
    58→      data = %{
    59→        test_cases:
    60→          Enum.map(test_cases, fn test_case ->
    61→            %{
    62→              id: test_case.id,
    63→              name: test_case.name,
    64→              module_name: test_case.module_name,
    65→              suite_name: test_case.suite_name,
    66→              is_flaky: test_case.is_flaky,
    67→              is_quarantined: test_case.is_quarantined,
    68→              last_status: to_string(test_case.last_status),
    69→              last_duration: test_case.last_duration,
    70→              last_ran_at: Formatter.iso8601(test_case.last_ran_at, naive: :utc),
    71→              avg_duration: test_case.avg_duration
    72→            }
    73→          end),
    74→        pagination_metadata: %{
    75→          has_next_page: meta.has_next_page?,
    76→          has_previous_page: meta.has_previous_page?,
    77→          total_count: meta.total_count,
    78→          total_pages: meta.total_pages,
    79→          current_page: meta.current_page,
    80→          page_size: meta.page_size
    81→        }
    82→      }
    83→
    84→      {:reply, Response.json(Response.tool(), data), frame}
    85→    end
    86→  end
    87→
    88→  defp integer_argument(value, _default) when is_integer(value) and value > 0, do: value
    89→  defp integer_argument(_value, default), do: default
    90→
    91→  defp build_filters(arguments) do
    92→    arguments
    93→    |> Map.take([:flaky, :quarantined, :module_name, :name, :suite_name])
    94→    |> Enum.reduce([], fn
    95→      {:flaky, value}, filters -> maybe_add_filter(filters, :is_flaky, value)
    96→      {:quarantined, value}, filters -> maybe_add_filter(filters, :is_quarantined, value)
    97→      {field, value}, filters -> maybe_add_filter(filters, field, value)
    98→    end)
    99→    |> Enum.reverse()
   100→  end
   101→
   102→  defp maybe_add_filter(filters, _field, nil), do: filters
   103→
   104→  defp maybe_add_filter(filters, field, true) when field in [:is_flaky, :is_quarantined],
   105→    do: [%{field: field, op: :==, value: true} | filters]
   106→
   107→  defp maybe_add_filter(filters, field, value), do: [%{field: field, op: :==, value: value} | filters]
   108→end
   109→

1→defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.GetTestCase do
     2→  @moduledoc """
     3→  Get detailed information about a test case including reliability and flakiness metrics.
     4→  """
     5→
     6→  use Hermes.Server.Component, type: :tool
     7→
     8→  alias Hermes.Server.Response
     9→  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.ToolSupport
    10→  alias Tuist.Tests
    11→  alias Tuist.Tests.Analytics
    12→
    13→  @authorization_action :read
    14→  @authorization_category :test
    15→
    16→  schema do
    17→    field :test_case_id, :string, description: "The UUID of the test case. Required when not using identifier lookup."
    18→
    19→    field :account_handle, :string,
    20→      description: "The account handle (organization or user). Required when looking up by identifier."
    21→
    22→    field :project_handle, :string, description: "The project handle. Required when looking up by identifier."
    23→
    24→    field :identifier, :string,
    25→      description:
    26→        "Test case identifier in Module/Suite/TestCase or Module/TestCase format. " <>
    27→          "Required when not using test_case_id. Must be combined with account_handle and project_handle."
    28→  end
    29→
    30→  @impl true
    31→  def execute(%{test_case_id: test_case_id}, frame) when is_binary(test_case_id) do
    32→    with {:ok, test_case} <-
    33→           ToolSupport.load_resource(
    34→             Tests.get_test_case_by_id(test_case_id),
    35→             "Test case not found: #{test_case_id}",
    36→             frame
    37→           ),
    38→         {:ok, _project} <-
    39→           ToolSupport.authorize_project_by_id(
    40→             frame,
    41→             test_case.project_id,
    42→             @authorization_action,
    43→             @authorization_category
    44→           ) do
    45→      reply_with_test_case(test_case, frame)
    46→    end
    47→  end
    48→
    49→  def execute(%{account_handle: account_handle, project_handle: project_handle, identifier: identifier}, frame)
    50→      when is_binary(account_handle) and is_binary(project_handle) and is_binary(identifier) do
    51→    with {:ok, {module_name, suite_name, name}} <- parse_identifier(identifier, frame),
    52→         {:ok, project} <-
    53→           ToolSupport.load_and_authorize_project_by_handle(
    54→             account_handle,
    55→             project_handle,
    56→             frame,
    57→             @authorization_action,
    58→             @authorization_category,
    59→             "You do not have access to project: #{account_handle}/#{project_handle}"
    60→           ),
    61→         {:ok, test_case} <- find_test_case_by_name(project.id, module_name, suite_name, name, frame) do
    62→      reply_with_test_case(test_case, frame)
    63→    end
    64→  end
    65→
    66→  def execute(_arguments, frame) do
    67→    ToolSupport.invalid_params(
    68→      "Provide either test_case_id or identifier with account_handle and project_handle.",
    69→      frame
    70→    )
    71→  end
    72→
    73→  defp parse_identifier(identifier, frame) do
    74→    case String.split(identifier, "/") do
    75→      [module_name, suite_name, name] -> {:ok, {module_name, suite_name, name}}
    76→      [module_name, name] -> {:ok, {module_name, nil, name}}
    77→      _ -> ToolSupport.invalid_params("Invalid identifier format. Use Module/Suite/TestCase or Module/TestCase.", frame)
    78→    end
    79→  end
    80→
    81→  defp find_test_case_by_name(project_id, module_name, suite_name, name, frame) do
    82→    filters =
    83→      [
    84→        %{field: :module_name, op: :==, value: module_name},
    85→        %{field: :name, op: :==, value: name}
    86→      ] ++ if(suite_name, do: [%{field: :suite_name, op: :==, value: suite_name}], else: [])
    87→
    88→    {test_cases, _meta} =
    89→      Tests.list_test_cases(project_id, %{
    90→        filters: filters,
    91→        page: 1,
    92→        page_size: 1
    93→      })
    94→
    95→    case test_cases do
    96→      [test_case | _] -> {:ok, test_case}
    97→      [] -> ToolSupport.invalid_params("Test case not found: #{module_name}/#{suite_name || name}", frame)
    98→    end
    99→  end
   100→
   101→  defp reply_with_test_case(test_case, frame) do
   102→    analytics = Analytics.test_case_analytics_by_id(test_case.id)
   103→    reliability_rate = Analytics.test_case_reliability_by_id(test_case.id, "main")
   104→    flakiness_rate = Analytics.get_test_case_flakiness_rate(test_case)
   105→
   106→    data = %{
   107→      id: test_case.id,
   108→      name: test_case.name,
   109→      module_name: test_case.module_name,
   110→      suite_name: test_case.suite_name,
   111→      is_flaky: test_case.is_flaky,
   112→      is_quarantined: test_case.is_quarantined,
   113→      last_status: to_string(test_case.last_status),
   114→      last_duration: test_case.last_duration,
   115→      avg_duration: test_case.avg_duration,
   116→      reliability_rate: reliability_rate,
   117→      flakiness_rate: flakiness_rate,
   118→      total_runs: analytics.total_count,
   119→      failed_runs: analytics.failed_count
   120→    }
   121→
   122→    {:reply, Response.json(Response.tool(), data), frame}
   123→  end
   124→end
   125→

1→defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.ToolSupport do
     2→  @moduledoc false
     3→
     4→  alias Hermes.MCP.Error
     5→  alias Tuist.MCP.Authorization
     6→  alias Tuist.Projects
     7→
     8→  def authenticated_subject(assigns) when is_map(assigns) do
     9→    Authorization.authenticated_subject(assigns)
    10→  end
    11→
    12→  def authorize_project(frame, project, action, category, message \\ "You do not have access to this resource.") do
    13→    if Authorization.authorize(authenticated_subject(frame.assigns), action, project, category) do
    14→      :ok
    15→    else
    16→      invalid_params(message, frame)
    17→    end
    18→  end
    19→
    20→  def load_resource({:ok, resource}, _message, _frame), do: {:ok, resource}
    21→  def load_resource({:error, :not_found}, message, frame), do: invalid_params(message, frame)
    22→  def load_resource(_result, message, frame), do: invalid_params(message, frame)
    23→
    24→  def load_project(project_id, frame, message \\ "Project not found.") do
    25→    case Projects.get_project_by_id(project_id) do
    26→      nil -> invalid_params(message, frame)
    27→      project -> {:ok, project}
    28→    end
    29→  end
    30→
    31→  def authorize_project_by_id(
    32→        frame,
    33→        project_id,
    34→        action,
    35→        category,
    36→        unauthorized_message \\ "You do not have access to this resource.",
    37→        not_found_message \\ "Project not found."
    38→      ) do
    39→    with {:ok, project} <- load_project(project_id, frame, not_found_message),
    40→         :ok <- authorize_project(frame, project, action, category, unauthorized_message) do
    41→      {:ok, project}
    42→    end
    43→  end
    44→
    45→  def load_project_by_handle(account_handle, project_handle, frame) do
    46→    case Projects.get_project_by_account_and_project_handles(account_handle, project_handle) do
    47→      nil -> invalid_params("Project not found: #{account_handle}/#{project_handle}", frame)
    48→      project -> {:ok, project}
    49→    end
    50→  end
    51→
    52→  def load_and_authorize_project_by_handle(account_handle, project_handle, frame, action, category, unauthorized_message) do
    53→    with {:ok, project} <- load_project_by_handle(account_handle, project_handle, frame),
    54→         :ok <- authorize_project(frame, project, action, category, unauthorized_message) do
    55→      {:ok, project}
    56→    end
    57→  end
    58→
    59→  def invalid_params(message, frame) do
    60→    {:error, Error.protocol(:invalid_params, %{message: message}), frame}
    61→  end
    62→end
    63→

1→defmodule TuistWeb.AuthenticationPlug do
     2→  @moduledoc """
     3→  A plug that deals with authentication of requests.
     4→  """
     5→  use TuistWeb, :controller
     6→
     7→  import Plug.Conn
     8→
     9→  alias Tuist.Accounts.AuthenticatedAccount
    10→  alias Tuist.Accounts.User
    11→  alias Tuist.Projects
    12→  alias Tuist.Projects.Project
    13→  alias TuistWeb.Headers
    14→  alias TuistWeb.RequestOrigin
    15→  alias TuistWeb.WarningsHeaderPlug
    16→
    17→  @mcp_resource_metadata_path "/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource/mcp"
    18→
    19→  def init(:load_authenticated_subject = opts), do: opts
    20→  def init({:require_authentication, _} = opts), do: opts
    21→
    22→  def call(conn, :load_authenticated_subject) do
    23→    token = TuistWeb.Authentication.get_authorization_token_from_conn(conn)
    24→
    25→    if token do
    26→      get_authenticated_subject(conn, token)
    27→    else
    28→      conn
    29→    end
    30→  end
    31→
    32→  def call(conn, {:require_authentication, opts}) do
    33→    response_type = Keyword.get(opts, :response_type, :open_api)
    34→
    35→    if TuistWeb.Authentication.authenticated?(conn) do
    36→      conn
    37→    else
    38→      case response_type do
    39→        :open_api ->
    40→          conn
    41→          |> put_status(:unauthorized)
    42→          |> json(%{message: "You need to be authenticated to access this resource."})
    43→          |> halt()
    44→
    45→        :mcp ->
    46→          conn
    47→          |> put_resp_header(
    48→            "www-authenticate",
    49→            ~s(Bearer realm="tuist-mcp", resource_metadata="#{RequestOrigin.from_conn(conn)}#{@mcp_resource_metadata_path}")
    50→          )
    51→          |> put_status(:unauthorized)
    52→          |> json(%{
    53→            error: "invalid_token",
    54→            error_description: "Missing or invalid access token."
    55→          })
    56→          |> halt()
    57→      end
    58→    end
    59→  end
    60→
    61→  defp get_authenticated_subject(conn, token) do
    62→    cache_key = [Atom.to_string(__MODULE__), "authenticated_subject", token]
    63→
    64→    cache_opts = [
    65→      ttl: Map.get(conn.assigns, :cache_ttl, to_timeout(minute: 1)),
    66→      cache: Map.get(conn.assigns, :cache, :tuist),
    67→      locking: true
    68→    ]
    69→
    70→    get_authenticated_subject = fn ->
    71→      Tuist.Authentication.authenticated_subject(token)
    72→    end
    73→
    74→    authenticated_subject =
    75→      if Map.get(conn.assigns, :caching, false) do
    76→        Tuist.KeyValueStore.get_or_update(cache_key, cache_opts, get_authenticated_subject)
    77→      else
    78→        get_authenticated_subject.()
    79→      end
    80→
    81→    case authenticated_subject do
    82→      %Project{} = project ->
    83→        %{account: account} = project
    84→
    85→        cli_version = Headers.get_cli_version(conn)
    86→
    87→        conn =
    88→          if Projects.legacy_token?(token) and not is_nil(cli_version) and
    89→               Version.compare(cli_version, Version.parse!("4.20.0")) == :gt do
    90→            WarningsHeaderPlug.put_warning(
    91→              conn,
    92→              "The project token you are using is deprecated. Please create a new token by running `tuist projects token create #{account.name}/#{project.name}."
    93→            )
    94→          else
    95→            conn
    96→          end
    97→
    98→        TuistWeb.Authentication.put_current_project(conn, project)
    99→
   100→      %User{} = user ->

100→      %User{} = user ->
   101→        TuistWeb.Authentication.put_current_user(conn, user)
   102→
   103→      %AuthenticatedAccount{} = authenticated_account ->
   104→        assign(conn, :current_subject, authenticated_account)
   105→
   106→      nil ->
   107→        conn
   108→    end
   109→  end
   110→end
   111→

1→defmodule TuistWeb.MCPControllerTest do
     2→  use TuistTestSupport.Cases.ConnCase, async: true
     3→  use Mimic
     4→
     5→  alias TuistTestSupport.Fixtures.AccountsFixtures
     6→  alias TuistWeb.RateLimit
     7→
     8→  describe "POST /mcp" do
     9→    test "returns a bearer challenge when not authenticated", %{conn: conn} do
    10→      conn = post_mcp(conn, %{})
    11→      response = json_response(conn, 401)
    12→
    13→      [www_authenticate] = get_resp_header(conn, "www-authenticate")
    14→
    15→      assert www_authenticate ==
    16→               ~s(Bearer realm="tuist-mcp", resource_metadata="http://www.example.com/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource/mcp")
    17→
    18→      assert response == %{
    19→               "error" => "invalid_token",
    20→               "error_description" => "Missing or invalid access token."
    21→             }
    22→    end
    23→
    24→    test "returns 202 for initialize over streamable transport", %{conn: conn} do
    25→      user = AccountsFixtures.user_fixture()
    26→      stub(RateLimit.MCP, :hit, fn _conn -> {:allow, 1} end)
    27→
    28→      conn =
    29→        conn
    30→        |> authenticated_mcp_conn(user.token)
    31→        |> post_mcp(%{
    32→          "jsonrpc" => "2.0",
    33→          "id" => 1,
    34→          "method" => "initialize",
    35→          "params" => %{}
    36→        })
    37→
    38→      assert conn.status == 202
    39→    end
    40→
    41→    test "returns protocol error when session is not initialized", %{conn: conn} do
    42→      user = AccountsFixtures.user_fixture()
    43→      stub(RateLimit.MCP, :hit, fn _conn -> {:allow, 1} end)
    44→
    45→      conn =
    46→        conn
    47→        |> authenticated_mcp_conn(user.token)
    48→        |> post_mcp(%{
    49→          "jsonrpc" => "2.0",
    50→          "id" => 2,
    51→          "method" => "tools/list",
    52→          "params" => %{}
    53→        })
    54→
    55→      response = json_response(conn, 200)
    56→      assert response["jsonrpc"] == "2.0"
    57→      assert is_binary(response["id"])
    58→      assert response["error"]["code"] == -32_600
    59→      assert response["error"]["message"] == "Invalid Request"
    60→    end
    61→
    62→    test "returns 202 for notifications", %{conn: conn} do
    63→      user = AccountsFixtures.user_fixture()
    64→      stub(RateLimit.MCP, :hit, fn _conn -> {:allow, 1} end)
    65→
    66→      conn =
    67→        conn
    68→        |> authenticated_mcp_conn(user.token)
    69→        |> post_mcp(%{
    70→          "jsonrpc" => "2.0",
    71→          "method" => "notifications/initialized",
    72→          "params" => %{}
    73→        })
    74→
    75→      assert conn.status == 202
    76→    end
    77→
    78→    test "returns rate limit error when rate limited", %{conn: conn} do
    79→      user = AccountsFixtures.user_fixture()
    80→      stub(RateLimit.MCP, :hit, fn _conn -> {:deny, 0} end)
    81→
    82→      conn =
    83→        conn
    84→        |> put_req_header("authorization", "Bearer #{user.token}")
    85→        |> post_mcp(%{
    86→          "jsonrpc" => "2.0",
    87→          "id" => 5,
    88→          "method" => "initialize",
    89→          "params" => %{}
    90→        })
    91→
    92→      response = json_response(conn, 429)
    93→      assert response["error"]["code"] == -32_603
    94→      assert response["error"]["message"] =~ "Rate limit"
    95→    end
    96→  end
    97→
    98→  defp post_mcp(conn, body) do
    99→    conn
   100→    |> put_req_header("content-type", "application/json")
   101→    |> post("/mcp", Jason.encode!(body))
   102→  end
   103→
   104→  defp authenticated_mcp_conn(conn, token) do
   105→    conn
   106→    |> put_req_header("authorization", "Bearer #{token}")
   107→    |> put_req_header("accept", "application/json, text/event-stream")
   108→  end
   109→end
   110→

130→  end
   131→
   132→  pipeline :non_authenticated_api do
   133→    plug :accepts, ["json"]
   134→
   135→    plug TuistWeb.WarningsHeaderPlug
   136→  end
   137→
   138→  pipeline :mcp do
   139→    plug TuistWeb.AuthenticationPlug, :load_authenticated_subject
   140→    plug TuistWeb.AuthenticationPlug, {:require_authentication, response_type: :mcp}
   141→    plug TuistWeb.Plugs.MCPRateLimitPlug
   142→  end
   143→
   144→  pipeline :authenticated_api do
   145→    plug :accepts, ["json", "application/octet-stream"]
   146→
   147→    plug TuistWeb.WarningsHeaderPlug
   148→    plug TuistWeb.AuthenticationPlug, :load_authenticated_subject
   149→    plug TuistWeb.AuthenticationPlug, {:require_authentication, response_type: :open_api}
   150→    plug SentryContextPlug
   151→    plug ObservabilityContextPlug
   152→  end
   153→
   154→  pipeline :authenticated do
   155→    plug TuistWeb.AuthenticationPlug, :load_authenticated_subject
   156→    plug SentryContextPlug
   157→    plug ObservabilityContextPlug
   158→  end
   159→
   160→  pipeline :on_premise_api do
   161→    plug TuistWeb.OnPremisePlug, :api_license_validation
   162→  end
   163→
   164→  pipeline :analytics do
   165→    plug TuistWeb.AnalyticsPlug, :track_page_view
   166→  end
   167→
   168→  # Marketing
   169→
   170→  scope "/" do
   171→    pipe_through [:browser_marketing_feed]
   172→
   173→    redirect("/rss.xml", "/blog/rss.xml", :permanent, preserve_query_string: true)
   174→    redirect("/case-studies", "/customers", :permanent, preserve_query_string: true)
   175→    redirect("/case-studies/:slug", "/customers/:slug", :permanent, preserve_query_string: true)
   176→
   177→    get "/blog/rss.xml", MarketingController, :blog_rss, metadata: %{type: :marketing}
   178→
   179→    get "/blog/atom.xml", MarketingController, :blog_atom, metadata: %{type: :marketing}

1→defmodule TuistWeb.WellKnownController do
     2→  use TuistWeb, :controller
     3→
     4→  alias Tuist.Environment
     5→  alias Tuist.Namespace.JWTToken
     6→  alias TuistWeb.RequestOrigin
     7→
     8→  @mcp_path "/mcp"
     9→  @oauth_token_path "/oauth2/token"
    10→  @oauth_authorize_path "/oauth2/authorize"
    11→  @oauth_registration_path "/oauth2/register"
    12→
    13→  @doc """
    14→  Returns the OpenID configuration for JWT verification.
    15→  """
    16→  def openid_configuration(conn, _params) do
    17→    issuer = JWTToken.issuer()
    18→
    19→    configuration = %{
    20→      issuer: issuer,
    21→      jwks_uri: "#{issuer}/.well-known/jwks.json",
    22→      response_types_supported: ["id_token"],
    23→      subject_types_supported: ["public"],
    24→      id_token_signing_alg_values_supported: ["RS256"],
    25→      claims_supported: ["iss", "sub", "aud", "exp", "iat"],
    26→      scopes_supported: ["openid"]
    27→    }
    28→
    29→    conn
    30→    |> put_resp_content_type("application/json")
    31→    |> json(configuration)
    32→  end
    33→
    34→  @doc """
    35→  Returns OAuth Authorization Server metadata.
    36→  """
    37→  def oauth_authorization_server(conn, _params) do
    38→    issuer = Environment.app_url()
    39→
    40→    configuration = %{
    41→      issuer: issuer,
    42→      authorization_endpoint: "#{issuer}#{@oauth_authorize_path}",
    43→      token_endpoint: "#{issuer}#{@oauth_token_path}",
    44→      registration_endpoint: "#{issuer}#{@oauth_registration_path}",
    45→      grant_types_supported: ["authorization_code", "refresh_token"],
    46→      response_types_supported: ["code"],
    47→      code_challenge_methods_supported: ["S256"],
    48→      scopes_supported: ["mcp"],
    49→      token_endpoint_auth_methods_supported: [
    50→        "none",
    51→        "client_secret_basic",
    52→        "client_secret_post",
    53→        "client_secret_jwt",
    54→        "private_key_jwt"

1→defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.Prompts.FixFlakyTest do
     2→  @moduledoc """
     3→  Guides you through fixing a flaky test by analyzing failure patterns, identifying the root cause, and applying a targeted correction.
     4→  """
     5→
     6→  use Hermes.Server.Component, type: :prompt
     7→
     8→  alias Hermes.Server.Response
     9→
    10→  schema do
    11→    field :account_handle, :string,
    12→      description: "The account handle (organization or user). Required if project_handle is provided."
    13→
    14→    field :project_handle, :string, description: "The project handle. Required if account_handle is provided."
    15→
    16→    field :test_case_id, :string,
    17→      description:
    18→        "The test case UUID or identifier (`Module/Suite/TestCase` or `Module/TestCase`) to fix. " <>
    19→          "When using an identifier, provide account_handle and project_handle."
    20→  end
    21→
    22→  @impl true
    23→  def get_messages(arguments, frame) do
    24→    account_handle = Map.get(arguments, :account_handle)
    25→    project_handle = Map.get(arguments, :project_handle)
    26→    test_case_id = Map.get(arguments, :test_case_id)
    27→
    28→    response =
    29→      Response.user_message(Response.prompt(), %{
    30→        "type" => "text",
    31→        "text" => prompt_text(account_handle, project_handle, test_case_id)
    32→      })
    33→
    34→    {:reply, response, frame}
    35→  end
    36→
    37→  defp prompt_text(account_handle, project_handle, test_case_id) do
    38→    discovery_section = discovery_section(account_handle, project_handle, test_case_id)
    39→
    40→    """
    41→    # Fix Flaky Test
    42→
    43→    Use MCP tools to discover flaky behavior, inspect failure details, and guide a targeted fix.
    44→
    45→    ## Available MCP tools
    46→
    47→    - **list_projects**: List all accessible projects.
    48→    - **list_test_cases**: List test cases for a project (requires account_handle and project_handle; use flaky=true to focus on flaky tests).
    49→    - **get_test_case**: Get detailed metrics for a test case (requires `test_case_id` or `identifier` with `account_handle` and `project_handle`).
    50→      `test_case_id` is the `id` field returned by `list_test_cases`.
    51→    - **get_test_run**: Get detailed metrics for a test run (requires test_run_id).
    52→    - **get_test_case_run**: Get failure details for a specific test case run (requires test_case_run_id).
    53→
    54→    ## Workflow
    55→
    56→    #{discovery_section}
    57→
    58→    ### 2. Get test case metrics
    59→
    60→    Use `get_test_case` with the test case ID (`list_test_cases[].id`), or with identifier + account/project handles. Key fields:
    61→
    62→    - **reliability_rate**: percentage of successful runs (higher is better)
    63→    - **flakiness_rate**: percentage of runs marked flaky in the last 30 days
    64→    - **total_runs** / **failed_runs**: volume context
    65→    - **last_status**: current state
    66→
    67→    ### 3. Correlate with related flaky tests
    68→
    69→    Use `list_test_cases` with focused filters such as `module_name`, `suite_name`, `name`, and `flaky=true` to inspect nearby failures in the same area.
    70→    Compare with `get_test_case` metrics to identify whether the issue is isolated or systematic.
    71→
    72→    Look for patterns:
    73→
    74→    - Does it fail on specific branches?
    75→    - Does it fail only in CI (`is_ci: true`)?
    76→    - Are failures clustered around specific commits?
    77→
    78→    ### 4. Get failure details for a run
    79→
    80→    Use `get_test_case_run` with a failing run ID. Key fields:
    81→
    82→    - **failures[].message**: the assertion or error message
    83→    - **failures[].path**: source file path
    84→    - **failures[].line_number**: exact line of failure
    85→    - **failures[].issue_type**: type of issue
    86→    - **repetitions**: retry behavior (pass/fail sequence)
    87→    - **test_run_id**: the broader test run this execution belongs to
    88→
    89→    ### 5. Read and analyze source code
    90→
    91→    Open the file at the reported path and line number. Read the full test function and its setup/teardown.
    92→
    93→    Common flaky patterns:
    94→
    95→    **Timing and async issues:**
    96→    - Missing waits for async operations. Fix: use await, expectations with timeouts, or polling.
    97→    - Race conditions with shared state. Fix: synchronize access or use serial queues.
    98→    - Hardcoded timeouts that are too short on CI. Fix: use condition-based waits.
    99→
   100→    **Shared state:**
   101→    - Test pollution from global/static state. Fix: reset state in setUp/tearDown.
   102→    - Singleton contamination. Fix: inject dependencies or reset singletons.
   103→    - File system leftovers. Fix: use temporary directories and clean up.
   104→
   105→    **Environment dependencies:**
   106→    - Network calls to real services. Fix: mock network calls.
   107→    - Date/time sensitivity. Fix: inject a clock or freeze time.
   108→    - Hardcoded file system paths. Fix: use relative paths or temp directories.
   109→
   110→    **Order dependence:**
   111→    - Implicit ordering between tests. Fix: make each test self-contained.
   112→    - Parallel execution conflicts. Fix: use unique resources per test.
   113→
   114→    ### 6. Reproduce the failure before changing code
   115→
   116→    Reproduce at least one failure first in your test environment.
   117→    If you cannot reproduce it, do not claim a fix. Instead:
   118→
   119→    - document that reproduction failed
   120→    - compare CI context from MCP data (`is_ci`, branch, commit, scheme)
   121→    - explain what additional signal is needed to proceed
   122→
   123→    ### 7. Apply the fix
   124→
   125→    - Apply the smallest fix that addresses the root cause.
   126→    - Do not refactor unrelated code.
   127→    - Reuse existing test utilities before creating new ones.
   128→
   129→    ### 8. Verify
   130→
   131→    Run the specific test repeatedly in your test environment until intermittent failures stop appearing.
   132→    Use 50-100 iterations for fast unit tests, and 2-5 iterations for slower integration tests.
   133→
   134→    ### Done checklist
   135→
   136→    - Identified the root cause of flakiness.
   137→    - Reproduced the flaky failure before applying the fix.
   138→    - Applied a targeted fix.
   139→    - Verified the test passes consistently across repeated runs.
   140→    - Did not introduce new shared state or hidden dependencies.
   141→    - Committed the fix with a descriptive message.
   142→    """
   143→  end
   144→
   145→  defp discovery_section(nil, nil, nil) do
   146→    """
   147→    ### 1. Discover flaky tests
   148→
   149→    1. Use `list_projects` to find available projects.
   150→    2. Use `list_test_cases` with account_handle and project_handle and set flaky=true.
   151→    3. Pick a flaky test case and use its `id` as `test_case_id` in `get_test_case`.
   152→    """
   153→  end
   154→
   155→  defp discovery_section(account_handle, project_handle, nil)
   156→       when is_binary(account_handle) and is_binary(project_handle) do
   157→    """
   158→    ### 1. Discover flaky tests
   159→
   160→    1. Use `list_test_cases` with account_handle="#{account_handle}" and project_handle="#{project_handle}" and set flaky=true.
   161→    2. Pick a flaky test case and use its `id` as `test_case_id` in `get_test_case`.
   162→    """
   163→  end
   164→
   165→  defp discovery_section(_account_handle, _project_handle, test_case_id) when is_binary(test_case_id) do
   166→    """
   167→    ### 1. Get test case metrics
   168→
   169→    Use `get_test_case` with test_case_id="#{test_case_id}".
   170→    """
   171→  end
   172→
   173→  defp discovery_section(_, _, _) do
   174→    discovery_section(nil, nil, nil)
   175→  end
   176→end
   177→

Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive view. Let me create a summary document with all the details:

## MCP Server Implementation Summary

I've thoroughly explored the MCP server implementation in the Tuist codebase. Here's a comprehensive breakdown:

### 1. **MCP Endpoint Routing**

**Location**: `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist_web/router.ex` (lines 318-322)

```elixir
scope "/" do
  pipe_through [:mcp]
  forward "/mcp", Hermes.Server.Transport.StreamableHTTP.Plug, server: Tuist.MCP.Server
end
```

The MCP endpoint is a simple HTTP forward to the `Hermes.Server.Transport.StreamableHTTP.Plug` (a library) which delegates to `Tuist.MCP.Server`.

**Pipeline**: `:mcp` (lines 138-142)
- Loads authenticated subject via `AuthenticationPlug`
- Requires authentication with response type `:mcp` (returns OAuth challenge)
- Applies MCP rate limiting via `MCPRateLimitPlug`

### 2. **Authentication Flow**

**File**: `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist_web/plugs/authentication_plug.ex`

The `:mcp` response type (lines 45-56) returns:
- **Status**: 401 Unauthorized
- **Header**: `www-authenticate` with OAuth 2.0 Bearer challenge
- **Body**: JSON with error details and resource metadata endpoint

```elixir
~s(Bearer realm="tuist-mcp", resource_metadata="#{RequestOrigin.from_conn(conn)}/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource/mcp")
```

Authenticated subjects can be:
- `User` (via user token)
- `Project` (via project token)
- `AuthenticatedAccount` (via OAuth)

### 3. **Rate Limiting**

**File**: `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist_web/plugs/mcp_rate_limit_plug.ex`

Delegates to `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist_web/rate_limit/mcp.ex`:

- Buckets requests by authenticated subject (user ID, project ID, or account ID)
- Returns 429 (Too Many Requests) with JSON-RPC error when rate limited:
```json
{
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "id": "...",
  "error": {
    "code": -32603,
    "message": "Rate limit exceeded. Please try again later."
  }
}
```

### 4. **MCP Server Core Implementation**

**File**: `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist/mcp/server.ex`

```elixir
use Hermes.Server,
  name: "tuist",
  version: "1.4.1",
  capabilities: [{:tools, list_changed?: false}, {:prompts, list_changed?: false}],
  protocol_versions: ["2025-03-26"]
```

**Registered Components**:
- **Tools**: `list_projects`, `list_test_cases`, `get_test_case`, `get_test_run`, `get_test_case_run`
- **Prompts**: `fix_flaky_test`

Custom request handlers for `tools/call` and `prompts/get` that ensure `arguments` parameter defaults to `{}` if missing (lines 31-50).

### 5. **List Projects Tool Implementation**

**File**: `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist/mcp/components/tools/list_projects.ex`

```elixir
defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListProjects do
  use Hermes.Server.Component, type: :tool
  
  schema do
    # No parameters required
  end

  def execute(_arguments, frame) do
    subject = Authorization.authenticated_subject(frame.assigns)
    projects = Projects.list_accessible_projects(subject, preload: [:account])
    
    data = Enum.map(projects, fn project ->
      %{
        id: project.id,
        name: project.name,
        account_handle: project.account.name,
        full_handle: "#{project.account.name}/#{project.name}"
      }
    end)
    
    {:reply, Response.json(Response.tool(), data), frame}
  end
end
```

**Request/Response Flow**:
1. Client sends JSON-RPC request with method `tools/call` and name `list_projects`
2. `Hermes` routes to the tool's `execute/2` function
3. Tool extracts authenticated subject from `frame.assigns` (set by `AuthenticationPlug`)
4. Tool calls `Projects.list_accessible_projects/2` to get authorized projects
5. Tool maps projects to response format
6. Tool returns `{:reply, Response.json(Response.tool(), data), frame}`
7. Hermes serializes as JSON-RPC response

### 6. **Authorization System**

**File**: `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist/mcp/authorization.ex`

```elixir
def authenticated_subject(assigns) when is_map(assigns) do
  assigns[:current_subject] || assigns[:current_user] || assigns[:current_project]
end

def authorize(subject, action, resource, category) do
  Authorization.authorize(:"#{category}_#{action}", subject, resource) == :ok
end
```

**ToolSupport Helper**: `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist/mcp/components/tool_support.ex`

Provides helpers for:
- Loading projects by ID or handle
- Authorizing projects for specific actions/categories
- Returning structured error responses

### 7. **Tool Pattern Example: ListTestCases**

**File**: `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist/mcp/components/tools/list_test_cases.ex`

More complex tools:
- Define schema with required/optional fields
- Load and authorize resources
- Apply authorization checks
- Return paginated results with metadata

### 8. **Prompt Implementation**

**File**: `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist/mcp/components/prompts/fix_flaky_test.ex`

```elixir
use Hermes.Server.Component, type: :prompt

def get_messages(arguments, frame) do
  response = Response.user_message(Response.prompt(), %{
    "type" => "text",
    "text" => prompt_text(account_handle, project_handle, test_case_id)
  })
  {:reply, response, frame}
end
```

Returns contextual prompt text that includes available MCP tools and workflows.

### 9. **Well-Known Endpoints**

**File**: `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist_web/controllers/well_known_controller.ex`

OAuth2 metadata endpoints (RFC 8414):
- `/.well-known/openid-configuration` - OpenID Connect config
- `/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server` - OAuth server metadata (includes `scopes_supported: ["mcp"]`)
- `/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource/mcp` - Protected resource metadata

MCP clients discover authentication requirements via these endpoints.

### 10. **Request/Response Flow Summary**

```
Client HTTP Request
  ↓
[TuistWeb.Router] POST /mcp
  ↓
[:mcp pipeline]
  ├─ AuthenticationPlug (load subject)
  ├─ AuthenticationPlug (require auth, return OAuth challenge if missing)
  └─ MCPRateLimitPlug (rate limit check)
  ↓
[Hermes.Server.Transport.StreamableHTTP.Plug]
  ↓
[Tuist.MCP.Server]
  ├─ Parse JSON-RPC request
  ├─ Custom handler for tools/call, prompts/get (ensure arguments present)
  ├─ Route to component (ListProjects, ListTestCases, etc.)
  ↓
[Component Execute Function]
  ├─ Extract authenticated_subject from frame.assigns
  ├─ Query database (Projects, Tests, etc.)
  ├─ Authorize if needed
  ├─ Format response
  └─ Return {:reply, Response.json(...), frame}
  ↓
[Hermes] JSON-RPC Response
  ↓
Client HTTP Response (200, 202, or error status)
```

All files are located in `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/` directory.

**Request/Response Flow**:
1. Client sends JSON-RPC request with method `tools/call` and name `list_projects`
2. `Hermes` routes to the tool's `execute/2` function
3. Tool extracts authenticated subject from `frame.assigns` (set by `AuthenticationPlug`)
4. Tool calls `Projects.list_accessible_projects/2` to get authorized projects
5. Tool maps projects to response format
6. Tool returns `{:reply, Response.json(Response.tool(), data), frame}`
7. Hermes serializes as JSON-RPC response

### 6. **Authorization System**

**File**: `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist/mcp/authorization.ex`

```elixir
def authenticated_subject(assigns) when is_map(assigns) do
  assigns[:current_subject] || assigns[:current_user] || assigns[:current_project]
end

def authorize(subject, action, resource, category) do
  Authorization.authorize(:"#{category}_#{action}", subject, resource) == :ok
end
```

**ToolSupport Helper**: `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist/mcp/components/tool_support.ex`

Provides helpers for:
- Loading projects by ID or handle
- Authorizing projects for specific actions/categories
- Returning structured error responses

### 7. **Tool Pattern Example: ListTestCases**

**File**: `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist/mcp/components/tools/list_test_cases.ex`

More complex tools:

### 8. **Prompt Implementation**

**File**: `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist/mcp/components/prompts/fix_flaky_test.ex`

Returns contextual prompt text that includes available MCP tools and workflows.

### 9. **Well-Known Endpoints**

**File**: `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist_web/controllers/well_known_controller.ex`

OAuth2 metadata endpoints (RFC 8414):
- `/.well-known/openid-configuration` - OpenID Connect config
- `/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server` - OAuth server metadata (includes `scopes_supported: ["mcp"]`)
- `/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource/mcp` - Protected resource metadata

MCP clients discover authentication requirements via these endpoints.

### 10. **Request/Response Flow Summary**

```
Client HTTP Request
  ↓
[TuistWeb.Router] POST /mcp
  ↓
[:mcp pipeline]
  ├─ AuthenticationPlug (load subject)
  ├─ AuthenticationPlug (require auth, return OAuth challenge if missing)
  └─ MCPRateLimitPlug (rate limit check)
  ↓
[Hermes.Server.Transport.StreamableHTTP.Plug]
  ↓
[Tuist.MCP.Server]
  ├─ Parse JSON-RPC request
  ├─ Custom handler for tools/call, prompts/get (ensure arguments present)
  ├─ Route to component (ListProjects, ListTestCases, etc.)
  ↓
[Component Execute Function]
  ├─ Extract authenticated_subject from frame.assigns
  ├─ Query database (Projects, Tests, etc.)
  ├─ Authorize if needed
  ├─ Format response
  └─ Return {:reply, Response.json(...), frame}
  ↓
[Hermes] JSON-RPC Response
  ↓
Client HTTP Response (200, 202, or error status)
```

1→if Code.ensure_loaded?(Plug) do
     2→  defmodule Hermes.Server.Transport.StreamableHTTP.Plug do
     3→    @moduledoc """
     4→    A Plug implementation for the Streamable HTTP transport.
     5→
     6→    This plug handles the MCP Streamable HTTP protocol as specified in MCP 2025-03-26.
     7→    It provides a single endpoint that supports both GET and POST methods:
     8→
     9→    - GET: Opens an SSE stream for server-to-client communication
    10→    - POST: Handles JSON-RPC messages from client to server
    11→    - DELETE: Closes a session
    12→
    13→    ## SSE Streaming Architecture
    14→
    15→    This Plug handles SSE streaming by keeping the request process alive 
    16→    and managing the streaming loop for server-to-client communication.
    17→
    18→    ## Usage in Phoenix Router
    19→
    20→        pipeline :mcp do
    21→          plug :accepts, ["json"]
    22→        end
    23→
    24→        scope "/mcp" do
    25→          pipe_through :mcp
    26→          forward "/", to: Hermes.Server.Transport.StreamableHTTP.Plug, server: :your_server_name
    27→        end
    28→
    29→    ## Usage in Plug Router
    30→
    31→        forward "/mcp", to: Hermes.Server.Transport.StreamableHTTP.Plug, init_opts: [server: :your_server_name]
    32→
    33→    ## Configuration Options
    34→
    35→    - `:server` - The server process name (required)
    36→    - `:session_header` - Custom header name for session ID (default: "mcp-session-id")
    37→    - `:timeout` - Request timeout in milliseconds (default: 30000)
    38→    - `:registry` - The registry to use. See `Hermes.Server.Registry.Adapter` for more information (default: Elixir's Registry implementation)
    39→
    40→    ## Security Features
    41→
    42→    - Origin header validation for DNS rebinding protection
    43→    - Session-based request validation
    44→    - Automatic session cleanup on connection loss
    45→    - Rate limiting support (when configured)
    46→
    47→    ## HTTP Response Codes
    48→
    49→    - 200: Successful request
    50→    - 202: Accepted (for notifications and responses)
    51→    - 400: Bad request (malformed JSON-RPC)
    52→    - 404: Session not found
    53→    - 405: Method not allowed
    54→    - 500: Internal server error
    55→    """
    56→
    57→    @behaviour Plug
    58→
    59→    use Hermes.Logging
    60→
    61→    import Plug.Conn
    62→
    63→    alias Hermes.MCP.Error
    64→    alias Hermes.MCP.ID
    65→    alias Hermes.MCP.Message
    66→    alias Hermes.Server.Transport.StreamableHTTP
    67→    alias Hermes.SSE.Streaming
    68→    alias Plug.Conn.Unfetched
    69→
    70→    require Message
    71→
    72→    @default_session_header "mcp-session-id"
    73→    @default_timeout 30_000
    74→
    75→    # Plug callbacks
    76→
    77→    @impl Plug
    78→    def init(opts) do
    79→      server = Keyword.fetch!(opts, :server)
    80→      registry = Keyword.get(opts, :registry, Hermes.Server.Registry)
    81→      transport = registry.transport(server, :streamable_http)
    82→      session_header = Keyword.get(opts, :session_header, @default_session_header)
    83→      timeout = Keyword.get(opts, :timeout, @default_timeout)
    84→
    85→      %{transport: transport, session_header: session_header, timeout: timeout}
    86→    end
    87→
    88→    @impl Plug
    89→    def call(conn, opts) do
    90→      case conn.method do
    91→        "GET" -> handle_get(conn, opts)
    92→        "POST" -> handle_post(conn, opts)
    93→        "DELETE" -> handle_delete(conn, opts)
    94→        _ -> send_error(conn, 405, "Method not allowed")
    95→      end
    96→    end
    97→
    98→    # GET request handler - establishes SSE connection
    99→
   100→    defp handle_get(conn, %{transport: transport, session_header: session_header}) do
   101→      if wants_sse?(conn) do
   102→        session_id = get_or_create_session_id(conn, session_header)
   103→
   104→        case StreamableHTTP.register_sse_handler(transport, session_id) do
   105→          :ok ->
   106→            start_sse_streaming(conn, transport, session_id, session_header)
   107→
   108→          {:error, reason} ->
   109→            Logging.transport_event("sse_registration_failed", %{reason: reason}, level: :error)
   110→
   111→            send_error(conn, 500, "Could not establish SSE connection")
   112→        end
   113→      else
   114→        send_error(conn, 406, "Accept header must include text/event-stream")
   115→      end
   116→    end
   117→
   118→    # POST request handler - processes MCP messages
   119→
   120→    defp handle_post(conn, %{transport: transport, session_header: session_header} = opts) do
   121→      with :ok <- validate_accept_header(conn),
   122→           {:ok, body, conn} <- maybe_read_request_body(conn, opts),
   123→           {:ok, [message]} <- maybe_parse_messages(body) do
   124→        session_id = determine_session_id(conn, session_header, message)
   125→        context = build_request_context(conn)
   126→
   127→        Logging.transport_event("parsed_messages", %{
   128→          message: message,
   129→          session_id: session_id
   130→        })
   131→
   132→        process_message(message, conn, transport, session_id, context, session_header)
   133→      else
   134→        {:error, :invalid_accept_header} ->
   135→          send_error(
   136→            conn,
   137→            406,
   138→            "Not Acceptable: Client must accept both application/json and text/event-stream"
   139→          )
   140→
   141→        {:error, :invalid_json} ->
   142→          send_jsonrpc_error(
   143→            conn,
   144→            Error.protocol(:parse_error, %{message: "Invalid JSON"}),
   145→            nil
   146→          )
   147→
   148→        {:error, reason} ->
   149→          Logging.transport_event("request_error", %{reason: reason}, level: :error)
   150→
   151→          send_jsonrpc_error(
   152→            conn,
   153→            Error.protocol(:parse_error, %{reason: reason}),
   154→            nil
   155→          )
   156→      end
   157→    end
   158→
   159→    defp process_message(message, conn, transport, session_id, context, session_header) when is_map(message) do
   160→      if Message.is_request(message) do
   161→        handle_request_with_possible_sse(
   162→          conn,
   163→          transport,
   164→          session_id,
   165→          message,
   166→          context,
   167→          session_header
   168→        )
   169→      else
   170→        # Notification
   171→        transport
   172→        |> StreamableHTTP.handle_message(session_id, message, context)
   173→        |> format_notification_response(conn)
   174→      end
   175→    end
   176→
   177→    defp format_notification_response({:ok, _}, conn) do
   178→      conn
   179→      |> put_resp_content_type("application/json")
   180→      |> send_resp(202, "{}")
   181→    end
   182→
   183→    defp format_notification_response({:error, %Error{} = error}, conn) do
   184→      send_jsonrpc_error(conn, error, nil)
   185→    end
   186→
   187→    defp format_notification_response({:error, reason}, conn) do
   188→      Logging.transport_event("notification_handling_failed", %{reason: reason}, level: :error)
   189→
   190→      send_jsonrpc_error(
   191→        conn,
   192→        Error.protocol(:internal_error, %{reason: reason}),
   193→        nil
   194→      )
   195→    end
   196→
   197→    # DELETE request handler - closes session
   198→
   199→    defp handle_delete(conn, %{transport: transport, session_header: session_header}) do
   200→      case get_req_header(conn, session_header) do
   201→        [session_id] when is_binary(session_id) and session_id != "" ->
   202→          StreamableHTTP.unregister_sse_handler(transport, session_id)
   203→
   204→          conn
   205→          |> put_resp_content_type("application/json")
   206→          |> send_resp(200, "{}")
   207→
   208→        _ ->
   209→          send_error(conn, 400, "Session ID required")
   210→      end
   211→    end
   212→
   213→    # Handle requests that might need SSE streaming
   214→
   215→    defp handle_request_with_possible_sse(conn, transport, session_id, body, context, session_header) do
   216→      if wants_sse?(conn) do
   217→        handle_sse_request(
   218→          conn,
   219→          transport,
   220→          session_id,
   221→          body,
   222→          context,
   223→          session_header
   224→        )
   225→      else
   226→        handle_json_request(
   227→          conn,
   228→          transport,
   229→          session_id,
   230→          body,
   231→          context,
   232→          session_header
   233→        )
   234→      end
   235→    end
   236→
   237→    defp handle_sse_request(conn, transport, session_id, body, context, session_header) do
   238→      case StreamableHTTP.handle_message_for_sse(
   239→             transport,
   240→             session_id,
   241→             body,
   242→             context
   243→           ) do
   244→        {:sse, response} ->
   245→          route_sse_response(
   246→            conn,
   247→            transport,
   248→            session_id,
   249→            response,
   250→            body,
   251→            context,
   252→            session_header
   253→          )
   254→
   255→        {:ok, response} ->
   256→          conn
   257→          |> put_resp_content_type("application/json")
   258→          |> maybe_add_session_header(session_header, session_id)
   259→          |> send_resp(200, response)
   260→
   261→        {:error, error} ->
   262→          handle_request_error(conn, error, body)
   263→      end
   264→    end
   265→
   266→    defp handle_json_request(conn, transport, session_id, body, context, session_header) do
   267→      case StreamableHTTP.handle_message(transport, session_id, body, context) do
   268→        {:ok, response} ->
   269→          conn
   270→          |> put_resp_content_type("application/json")
   271→          |> maybe_add_session_header(session_header, session_id)
   272→          |> send_resp(200, response)
   273→
   274→        {:error, error} ->
   275→          handle_request_error(conn, error, body)
   276→      end
   277→    end
   278→
   279→    defp route_sse_response(conn, transport, session_id, response, body, context, session_header) do
   280→      if handler_pid = StreamableHTTP.get_sse_handler(transport, session_id) do
   281→        send(handler_pid, {:sse_message, response})
   282→
   283→        conn
   284→        |> put_resp_content_type("application/json")
   285→        |> send_resp(202, "{}")
   286→      else
   287→        establish_sse_for_request(
   288→          conn,
   289→          transport,
   290→          session_id,
   291→          body,
   292→          context,
   293→          session_header
   294→        )
   295→      end
   296→    end
   297→
   298→    defp handle_request_error(conn, %Error{} = error, body) do
   299→      send_jsonrpc_error(conn, error, extract_request_id(body))
   300→    end
   301→
   302→    defp handle_request_error(conn, reason, body) do
   303→      Logging.transport_event("request_error", %{reason: reason}, level: :error)
   304→
   305→      send_jsonrpc_error(
   306→        conn,
   307→        Error.protocol(:internal_error, %{reason: reason}),
   308→        extract_request_id(body)
   309→      )
   310→    end
   311→
   312→    defp establish_sse_for_request(conn, transport, session_id, body, context, session_header) do
   313→      case StreamableHTTP.register_sse_handler(transport, session_id) do
   314→        :ok ->
   315→          start_background_request(transport, session_id, body, context)
   316→          start_sse_streaming(conn, transport, session_id, session_header)
   317→
   318→        {:error, reason} ->
   319→          Logging.transport_event("sse_registration_failed", %{reason: reason}, level: :error)
   320→
   321→          send_jsonrpc_error(
   322→            conn,
   323→            Error.protocol(:internal_error, %{reason: reason}),
   324→            extract_request_id(body)
   325→          )
   326→      end
   327→    end
   328→
   329→    defp start_background_request(transport, session_id, body, context) do
   330→      self_pid = self()
   331→
   332→      Task.start(fn ->
   333→        case StreamableHTTP.handle_message(transport, session_id, body, context) do
   334→          {:ok, response} when is_binary(response) ->
   335→            send(self_pid, {:sse_message, response})
   336→
   337→          {:error, reason} ->
   338→            Logging.transport_event(
   339→              "sse_background_request_error",
   340→              %{reason: reason},
   341→              level: :error
   342→            )
   343→        end
   344→      end)
   345→    end
   346→
   347→    defp start_sse_streaming(conn, transport, session_id, session_header) do
   348→      conn
   349→      |> put_resp_header(session_header, session_id)
   350→      |> Streaming.prepare_connection()
   351→      |> Streaming.start(transport, session_id,
   352→        on_close: fn ->
   353→          StreamableHTTP.unregister_sse_handler(transport, session_id)
   354→        end
   355→      )
   356→    end
   357→
   358→    # Helper functions
   359→
   360→    defp wants_sse?(conn) do
   361→      conn
   362→      |> get_req_header("accept")
   363→      |> List.first("")
   364→      |> String.contains?("text/event-stream")
   365→    end
   366→
   367→    defp validate_accept_header(conn) do
   368→      accept_header =
   369→        conn
   370→        |> get_req_header("accept")
   371→        |> List.first("")
   372→
   373→      if String.contains?(accept_header, "application/json") and
   374→           String.contains?(accept_header, "text/event-stream") do
   375→        :ok
   376→      else
   377→        {:error, :invalid_accept_header}
   378→      end
   379→    end
   380→
   381→    defp get_or_create_session_id(conn, session_header) do
   382→      case get_req_header(conn, session_header) do
   383→        [session_id] when is_binary(session_id) and session_id != "" ->
   384→          session_id
   385→
   386→        _ ->
   387→          ID.generate_session_id()
   388→      end
   389→    end
   390→
   391→    defp determine_session_id(_conn, _header, [message]) when Message.is_initialize(message) do
   392→      ID.generate_session_id()
   393→    end
   394→
   395→    defp determine_session_id(conn, session_header, _message) do
   396→      get_or_create_session_id(conn, session_header)
   397→    end
   398→
   399→    defp maybe_parse_messages(body) when is_binary(body) do
   400→      case Message.decode(body) do
   401→        {:ok, messages} ->
   402→          {:ok, messages}
   403→
   404→        {:error, reason} ->
   405→          Logging.transport_event(
   406→            "parse_error",
   407→            %{body: body, reason: inspect(reason)},
   408→            level: :error
   409→          )
   410→
   411→          {:error, :invalid_json}
   412→      end
   413→    end
   414→
   415→    defp maybe_parse_messages(body) when is_map(body) do
   416→      case Message.validate_message(body) do
   417→        {:ok, message} -> {:ok, [message]}
   418→        {:error, _} -> {:error, :invalid_json}
   419→      end
   420→    end
   421→
   422→    defp maybe_add_session_header(conn, session_header, session_id) do
   423→      if get_req_header(conn, session_header) == [] do
   424→        put_resp_header(conn, session_header, session_id)
   425→      else
   426→        conn
   427→      end
   428→    end
   429→
   430→    defp maybe_read_request_body(%{body_params: %Unfetched{aspect: :body_params}} = conn, %{timeout: timeout}) do
   431→      case Plug.Conn.read_body(conn, read_timeout: timeout) do
   432→        {:ok, body, conn} -> {:ok, body, conn}
   433→        {:error, reason} -> {:error, reason}
   434→      end
   435→    end
   436→
   437→    defp maybe_read_request_body(%{body_params: body} = conn, _), do: {:ok, body, conn}
   438→
   439→    defp send_error(conn, status, message) do
   440→      data = %{data: %{message: message, http_status: status}}
   441→
   442→      mcp_error =
   443→        case status do
   444→          405 -> Error.protocol(:method_not_found, data)
   445→          406 -> Error.protocol(:invalid_request, data)
   446→          _ -> Error.protocol(:internal_error, data)
   447→        end
   448→
   449→      {:ok, error_response} = Error.to_json_rpc(mcp_error, ID.generate_error_id())
   450→
   451→      conn
   452→      |> put_resp_content_type("application/json")
   453→      |> send_resp(status, error_response)
   454→    end
   455→
   456→    defp send_jsonrpc_error(conn, %Error{} = error, id) do
   457→      error_id = id || ID.generate_error_id()
   458→      {:ok, encoded_error} = Error.to_json_rpc(error, error_id)
   459→
   460→      conn
   461→      |> put_resp_content_type("application/json")
   462→      |> send_resp(400, encoded_error)
   463→    end
   464→
   465→    defp extract_request_id(%{"id" => request_id}), do: request_id
   466→    defp extract_request_id(_), do: nil
   467→
   468→    defp build_request_context(conn) do
   469→      %{
   470→        assigns: conn.assigns,
   471→        type: :http,
   472→        req_headers: conn.req_headers,
   473→        query_params: fetch_query_params_safe(conn),
   474→        remote_ip: conn.remote_ip,
   475→        scheme: conn.scheme,
   476→        host: conn.host,
   477→        port: conn.port,
   478→        request_path: conn.request_path
   479→      }
   480→    end
   481→
   482→    defp fetch_query_params_safe(conn) do
   483→      case conn.query_params do
   484→        %Unfetched{} -> nil
   485→        params -> params
   486→      end
   487→    end
   488→  end
   489→end

if Code.ensure_loaded?(Plug) do
  defmodule Hermes.Server.Transport.StreamableHTTP.Plug do
    @moduledoc """
    A Plug implementation for the Streamable HTTP transport.

    This plug handles the MCP Streamable HTTP protocol as specified in MCP 2025-03-26.
    It provides a single endpoint that supports both GET and POST methods:

    - GET: Opens an SSE stream for server-to-client communication
    - POST: Handles JSON-RPC messages from client to server
    - DELETE: Closes a session

    ## SSE Streaming Architecture

    This Plug handles SSE streaming by keeping the request process alive 
    and managing the streaming loop for server-to-client communication.

    ## Usage in Phoenix Router

        pipeline :mcp do
          plug :accepts, ["json"]
        end

        scope "/mcp" do
          pipe_through :mcp
          forward "/", to: Hermes.Server.Transport.StreamableHTTP.Plug, server: :your_server_name
        end

    ## Usage in Plug Router

        forward "/mcp", to: Hermes.Server.Transport.StreamableHTTP.Plug, init_opts: [server: :your_server_name]

    ## Configuration Options

    - `:server` - The server process name (required)
    - `:session_header` - Custom header name for session ID (default: "mcp-session-id")
    - `:timeout` - Request timeout in milliseconds (default: 30000)
    - `:registry` - The registry to use. See `Hermes.Server.Registry.Adapter` for more information (default: Elixir's Registry implementation)

    ## Security Features

    - Origin header validation for DNS rebinding protection
    - Session-based request validation
    - Automatic session cleanup on connection loss
    - Rate limiting support (when configured)

    ## HTTP Response Codes

    - 200: Successful request
    - 202: Accepted (for notifications and responses)
    - 400: Bad request (malformed JSON-RPC)
    - 404: Session not found
    - 405: Method not allowed
    - 500: Internal server error
    """

    @behaviour Plug

    use Hermes.Logging

    import Plug.Conn

    alias Hermes.MCP.Error
    alias Hermes.MCP.ID
    alias Hermes.MCP.Message
    alias Hermes.Server.Transport.StreamableHTTP
    alias Hermes.SSE.Streaming
    alias Plug.Conn.Unfetched

    require Message

    @default_session_header "mcp-session-id"
    @default_timeout 30_000

    # Plug callbacks

    @impl Plug
    def init(opts) do
      server = Keyword.fetch!(opts, :server)
      registry = Keyword.get(opts, :registry, Hermes.Server.Registry)
      transport = registry.transport(server, :streamable_http)
      session_header = Keyword.get(opts, :session_header, @default_session_header)
      timeout = Keyword.get(opts, :timeout, @default_timeout)

      %{transport: transport, session_header: session_header, timeout: timeout}
    end

    @impl Plug
    def call(conn, opts) do
      case conn.method do
        "GET" -> handle_get(conn, opts)
        "POST" -> handle_post(conn, opts)
        "DELETE" -> handle_delete(conn, opts)
        _ -> send_error(conn, 405, "Method not allowed")
      end
    end

    # GET request handler - establishes SSE connection

    defp handle_get(conn, %{transport: transport, session_header: session_header}) do
      if wants_sse?(conn) do
        session_id = get_or_create_session_id(conn, session_header)

        case StreamableHTTP.register_sse_handler(transport, session_id) do
          :ok ->
            start_sse_streaming(conn, transport, session_id, session_header)

          {:error, reason} ->
            Logging.transport_event("sse_registration_failed", %{reason: reason}, level: :error)

            send_error(conn, 500, "Could not establish SSE connection")
        end
      else
        send_error(conn, 406, "Accept header must include text/event-stream")
      end
    end

    # POST request handler - processes MCP messages

    defp handle_post(conn, %{transport: transport, session_header: session_header} = opts) do
      with :ok <- validate_accept_header(conn),
           {:ok, body, conn} <- maybe_read_request_body(conn, opts),
           {:ok, [message]} <- maybe_parse_messages(body) do
        session_id = determine_session_id(conn, session_header, message)
        context = build_request_context(conn)

        Logging.transport_event("parsed_messages", %{
          message: message,
          session_id: session_id
        })

        process_message(message, conn, transport, session_id, context, session_header)
      else
        {:error, :invalid_accept_header} ->
          send_error(
            conn,
            406,
            "Not Acceptable: Client must accept both application/json and text/event-stream"
          )

        {:error, :invalid_json} ->
          send_jsonrpc_error(
            conn,
            Error.protocol(:parse_error, %{message: "Invalid JSON"}),
            nil
          )

        {:error, reason} ->
          Logging.transport_event("request_error", %{reason: reason}, level: :error)

          send_jsonrpc_error(
            conn,
            Error.protocol(:parse_error, %{reason: reason}),
            nil
          )
      end
    end

    defp process_message(message, conn, transport, session_id, context, session_header) when is_map(message) do
      if Message.is_request(message) do
        handle_request_with_possible_sse(
          conn,
          transport,
          session_id,
          message,
          context,
          session_header
        )
      else
        # Notification
        transport
        |> StreamableHTTP.handle_message(session_id, message, context)
        |> format_notification_response(conn)
      end
    end

    defp format_notification_response({:ok, _}, conn) do
      conn
      |> put_resp_content_type("application/json")
      |> send_resp(202, "{}")
    end

    defp format_notification_response({:error, %Error{} = error}, conn) do
      send_jsonrpc_error(conn, error, nil)
    end

    defp format_notification_response({:error, reason}, conn) do
      Logging.transport_event("notification_handling_failed", %{reason: reason}, level: :error)

      send_jsonrpc_error(
        conn,
        Error.protocol(:internal_error, %{reason: reason}),
        nil
      )
    end

    # DELETE request handler - closes session

    defp handle_delete(conn, %{transport: transport, session_header: session_header}) do
      case get_req_header(conn, session_header) do
        [session_id] when is_binary(session_id) and session_id != "" ->
          StreamableHTTP.unregister_sse_handler(transport, session_id)

          conn
          |> put_resp_content_type("application/json")
          |> send_resp(200, "{}")

        _ ->
          send_error(conn, 400, "Session ID required")
      end
    end

    # Handle requests that might need SSE streaming

    defp handle_request_with_possible_sse(conn, transport, session_id, body, context, session_header) do
      if wants_sse?(conn) do
        handle_sse_request(
          conn,
          transport,
          session_id,
          body,
          context,
          session_header
        )
      else
        handle_json_request(
          conn,
          transport,
          session_id,
          body,
          context,
          session_header
        )
      end
    end

    defp handle_sse_request(conn, transport, session_id, body, context, session_header) do
      case StreamableHTTP.handle_message_for_sse(
             transport,
             session_id,
             body,
             context
           ) do
        {:sse, response} ->
          route_sse_response(
            conn,
            transport,
            session_id,
            response,
            body,
            context,
            session_header
          )

        {:ok, response} ->
          conn
          |> put_resp_content_type("application/json")
          |> maybe_add_session_header(session_header, session_id)
          |> send_resp(200, response)

        {:error, error} ->
          handle_request_error(conn, error, body)
      end
    end

    defp handle_json_request(conn, transport, session_id, body, context, session_header) do
      case StreamableHTTP.handle_message(transport, session_id, body, context) do
        {:ok, response} ->
          conn
          |> put_resp_content_type("application/json")
          |> maybe_add_session_header(session_header, session_id)
          |> send_resp(200, response)

        {:error, error} ->
          handle_request_error(conn, error, body)
      end
    end

    defp route_sse_response(conn, transport, session_id, response, body, context, session_header) do
      if handler_pid = StreamableHTTP.get_sse_handler(transport, session_id) do
        send(handler_pid, {:sse_message, response})

        conn
        |> put_resp_content_type("application/json")
        |> send_resp(202, "{}")
      else
        establish_sse_for_request(
          conn,
          transport,
          session_id,
          body,
          context,
          session_header
        )
      end
    end

    defp handle_request_error(conn, %Error{} = error, body) do
      send_jsonrpc_error(conn, error, extract_request_id(body))
    end

    defp handle_request_error(conn, reason, body) do
      Logging.transport_event("request_error", %{reason: reason}, level: :error)

      send_jsonrpc_error(
        conn,
        Error.protocol(:internal_error, %{reason: reason}),
        extract_request_id(body)
      )
    end

    defp establish_sse_for_request(conn, transport, session_id, body, context, session_header) do
      case StreamableHTTP.register_sse_handler(transport, session_id) do
        :ok ->
          start_background_request(transport, session_id, body, context)
          start_sse_streaming(conn, transport, session_id, session_header)

        {:error, reason} ->
          Logging.transport_event("sse_registration_failed", %{reason: reason}, level: :error)

          send_jsonrpc_error(
            conn,
            Error.protocol(:internal_error, %{reason: reason}),
            extract_request_id(body)
          )
      end
    end

    defp start_background_request(transport, session_id, body, context) do
      self_pid = self()

      Task.start(fn ->
        case StreamableHTTP.handle_message(transport, session_id, body, context) do
          {:ok, response} when is_binary(response) ->
            send(self_pid, {:sse_message, response})

          {:error, reason} ->
            Logging.transport_event(
              "sse_background_request_error",
              %{reason: reason},
              level: :error
            )
        end
      end)
    end

    defp start_sse_streaming(conn, transport, session_id, session_header) do
      conn
      |> put_resp_header(session_header, session_id)
      |> Streaming.prepare_connection()
      |> Streaming.start(transport, session_id,
        on_close: fn ->
          StreamableHTTP.unregister_sse_handler(transport, session_id)
        end
      )
    end

    # Helper functions

    defp wants_sse?(conn) do
      conn
      |> get_req_header("accept")
      |> List.first("")
      |> String.contains?("text/event-stream")
    end

    defp validate_accept_header(conn) do
      accept_header =
        conn
        |> get_req_header("accept")
        |> List.first("")

      if String.contains?(accept_header, "application/json") and
           String.contains?(accept_header, "text/event-stream") do
        :ok
      else
        {:error, :invalid_accept_header}
      end
    end

    defp get_or_create_session_id(conn, session_header) do
      case get_req_header(conn, session_header) do
        [session_id] when is_binary(session_id) and session_id != "" ->
          session_id

        _ ->
          ID.generate_session_id()
      end
    end

    defp determine_session_id(_conn, _header, [message]) when Message.is_initialize(message) do
      ID.generate_session_id()
    end

    defp determine_session_id(conn, session_header, _message) do
      get_or_create_session_id(conn, session_header)
    end

    defp maybe_parse_messages(body) when is_binary(body) do
      case Message.decode(body) do
        {:ok, messages} ->
          {:ok, messages}

        {:error, reason} ->
          Logging.transport_event(
            "parse_error",
            %{body: body, reason: inspect(reason)},
            level: :error
          )

          {:error, :invalid_json}
      end
    end

    defp maybe_parse_messages(body) when is_map(body) do
      case Message.validate_message(body) do
        {:ok, message} -> {:ok, [message]}
        {:error, _} -> {:error, :invalid_json}
      end
    end

    defp maybe_add_session_header(conn, session_header, session_id) do
      if get_req_header(conn, session_header) == [] do
        put_resp_header(conn, session_header, session_id)
      else
        conn
      end
    end

    defp maybe_read_request_body(%{body_params: %Unfetched{aspect: :body_params}} = conn, %{timeout: timeout}) do
      case Plug.Conn.read_body(conn, read_timeout: timeout) do
        {:ok, body, conn} -> {:ok, body, conn}
        {:error, reason} -> {:error, reason}
      end
    end

    defp maybe_read_request_body(%{body_params: body} = conn, _), do: {:ok, body, conn}

    defp send_error(conn, status, message) do
      data = %{data: %{message: message, http_status: status}}

      mcp_error =
        case status do
          405 -> Error.protocol(:method_not_found, data)
          406 -> Error.protocol(:invalid_request, data)
          _ -> Error.protocol(:internal_error, data)
        end

      {:ok, error_response} = Error.to_json_rpc(mcp_error, ID.generate_error_id())

      conn
      |> put_resp_content_type("application/json")
      |> send_resp(status, error_response)
    end

    defp send_jsonrpc_error(conn, %Error{} = error, id) do
      error_id = id || ID.generate_error_id()
      {:ok, encoded_error} = Error.to_json_rpc(error, error_id)

      conn
      |> put_resp_content_type("application/json")
      |> send_resp(400, encoded_error)
    end

    defp extract_request_id(%{"id" => request_id}), do: request_id
    defp extract_request_id(_), do: nil

    defp build_request_context(conn) do
      %{
        assigns: conn.assigns,
        type: :http,
        req_headers: conn.req_headers,
        query_params: fetch_query_params_safe(conn),
        remote_ip: conn.remote_ip,
        scheme: conn.scheme,
        host: conn.host,
        port: conn.port,
        request_path: conn.request_path
      }
    end

    defp fetch_query_params_safe(conn) do
      case conn.query_params do
        %Unfetched{} -> nil
        params -> params
      end
    end
  end
end
