1→defmodule Hermes.Server.Transport.StreamableHTTP do
     2→  @moduledoc """
     3→  StreamableHTTP transport implementation for MCP servers.
     4→
     5→  This module provides an HTTP-based transport layer that supports multiple
     6→  concurrent client sessions through Server-Sent Events (SSE). It enables
     7→  web-based MCP clients to communicate with the server using standard HTTP
     8→  protocols.
     9→
    10→  ## Features
    11→
    12→  - Multiple concurrent client sessions
    13→  - Server-Sent Events for real-time server-to-client communication
    14→  - HTTP POST endpoint for client-to-server messages
    15→  - Automatic session cleanup on disconnect
    16→  - Integration with Phoenix/Plug applications
    17→
    18→  ## Usage
    19→
    20→  StreamableHTTP is typically started through the server supervisor:
    21→
    22→      Hermes.Server.start_link(MyServer, [],
    23→        transport: :streamable_http,
    24→        streamable_http: [port: 4000]
    25→      )
    26→
    27→  For integration with existing Phoenix/Plug applications:
    28→
    29→      # In your router
    30→      forward "/mcp", Hermes.Server.Transport.StreamableHTTP.Plug,
    31→        server: MyApp.MCPServer
    32→
    33→  ## Message Flow
    34→
    35→  1. Client connects to `/sse` endpoint, receives a session ID
    36→  2. Client sends messages via POST to `/messages` with session ID header
    37→  3. Server responses are pushed through the SSE connection
    38→  4. Connection closes on client disconnect or server shutdown
    39→
    40→  ## Configuration
    41→
    42→  - `:port` - HTTP server port (default: 4000)
    43→  - `:server` - The MCP server process to connect to
    44→  - `:name` - Process registration name
    45→  """
    46→
    47→  @behaviour Hermes.Transport.Behaviour
    48→
    49→  use GenServer
    50→  use Hermes.Logging
    51→
    52→  import Peri
    53→
    54→  alias Hermes.MCP.Message
    55→  alias Hermes.Telemetry
    56→  alias Hermes.Transport.Behaviour, as: Transport
    57→
    58→  require Message
    59→
    60→  @type t :: GenServer.server()
    61→
    62→  @typedoc """
    63→  StreamableHTTP transport options
    64→
    65→  - `:server` - The server process (required)
    66→  - `:name` - Name for registering the GenServer (required)
    67→  """
    68→  @type option ::
    69→          {:server, GenServer.server()}
    70→          | {:name, GenServer.name()}
    71→          | GenServer.option()
    72→
    73→  defschema(:parse_options, [
    74→    {:server, {:required, Hermes.get_schema(:process_name)}},
    75→    {:name, {:required, {:custom, &Hermes.genserver_name/1}}},
    76→    {:registry, {:atom, {:default, Hermes.Server.Registry}}},
    77→    {:request_timeout, {:integer, {:default, to_timeout(second: 30)}}},
    78→    {:task_supervisor, {:required, {:custom, &Hermes.genserver_name/1}}}
    79→  ])
    80→
    81→  @doc """
    82→  Starts the StreamableHTTP transport.
    83→  """
    84→  @impl Transport
    85→  @spec start_link(Enumerable.t(option())) :: GenServer.on_start()
    86→  def start_link(opts) do
    87→    opts = parse_options!(opts)
    88→    {name, opts} = Keyword.pop!(opts, :name)
    89→
    90→    GenServer.start_link(__MODULE__, Map.new(opts), name: name)
    91→  end
    92→
    93→  @doc """
    94→  Sends a message to the client via the active SSE connection.
    95→
    96→  This function is used for server-initiated notifications.
    97→  It will broadcast to all active SSE connections.
    98→
    99→  ## Parameters
   100→    * `transport` - The transport process
   101→    * `message` - The message to send
   102→
   103→  ## Returns
   104→    * `:ok` if message was sent successfully
   105→    * `{:error, reason}` otherwise
   106→  """
   107→  @impl Transport
   108→  @spec send_message(GenServer.server(), binary(), keyword()) :: :ok | {:error, term()}
   109→  def send_message(transport, message, _opts \\ []) when is_binary(message) do
   110→    GenServer.call(transport, {:send_message, message})
   111→  end
   112→
   113→  @doc """
   114→  Shuts down the transport connection.
   115→
   116→  This terminates all active sessions managed by this transport.
   117→
   118→  ## Parameters
   119→    * `transport` - The transport process
   120→  """
   121→  @impl Transport
   122→  @spec shutdown(GenServer.server()) :: :ok
   123→  def shutdown(transport) do
   124→    GenServer.cast(transport, :shutdown)
   125→  end
   126→
   127→  @impl Transport
   128→  def supported_protocol_versions, do: ["2025-03-26", "2025-06-18"]
   129→
   130→  @doc """
   131→  Registers an SSE handler process for a session.
   132→
   133→  Called by the Plug when establishing an SSE connection.
   134→  The calling process becomes the SSE handler for the session.
   135→  """
   136→  @spec register_sse_handler(GenServer.server(), String.t()) ::
   137→          :ok | {:error, term()}
   138→  def register_sse_handler(transport, session_id) do
   139→    GenServer.call(transport, {:register_sse_handler, session_id, self()})
   140→  end
   141→
   142→  @doc """
   143→  Unregisters an SSE handler process for a session.
   144→
   145→  Called when the SSE connection is closed.
   146→  """
   147→  @spec unregister_sse_handler(GenServer.server(), String.t()) :: :ok
   148→  def unregister_sse_handler(transport, session_id) do
   149→    GenServer.cast(transport, {:unregister_sse_handler, session_id})
   150→  end
   151→
   152→  @doc """
   153→  Handles an incoming message from a client with request context.
   154→
   155→  Called by the Plug when a message is received via HTTP POST.
   156→  """
   157→  @spec handle_message(GenServer.server(), String.t(), map() | list(map), map()) ::
   158→          {:ok, binary() | nil} | {:error, term()}
   159→  def handle_message(transport, session_id, message, context) do
   160→    GenServer.call(transport, {:handle_message, session_id, message, context})
   161→  end
   162→
   163→  @doc """
   164→  Handles an incoming message with context and returns {:sse, response} if SSE handler exists.
   165→
   166→  This allows the Plug to know whether to stream the response via SSE
   167→  or return it as a regular HTTP response.
   168→  """
   169→  @spec handle_message_for_sse(GenServer.server(), String.t(), map(), map()) ::
   170→          {:ok, binary()} | {:sse, binary()} | {:error, term()}
   171→  def handle_message_for_sse(transport, session_id, message, context) do
   172→    GenServer.call(
   173→      transport,
   174→      {:handle_message_for_sse, session_id, message, context}
   175→    )
   176→  end
   177→
   178→  @doc """
   179→  Gets the SSE handler process for a session.
   180→
   181→  Returns the pid of the process handling SSE for this session,
   182→  or nil if no SSE connection exists.
   183→  """
   184→  @spec get_sse_handler(GenServer.server(), String.t()) :: pid() | nil
   185→  def get_sse_handler(transport, session_id) do
   186→    GenServer.call(transport, {:get_sse_handler, session_id})
   187→  end
   188→
   189→  @doc """
   190→  Routes a message to a specific session's SSE handler.
   191→
   192→  Used for targeted server notifications to specific clients.
   193→  """
   194→  @spec route_to_session(GenServer.server(), String.t(), binary()) ::
   195→          :ok | {:error, term()}
   196→  def route_to_session(transport, session_id, message) do
   197→    GenServer.call(transport, {:route_to_session, session_id, message})
   198→  end
   199→
   200→  # GenServer implementation
   201→
   202→  @impl GenServer
   203→  def init(%{server: server} = opts) do
   204→    Process.flag(:trap_exit, true)
   205→
   206→    state = %{
   207→      server: server,
   208→      registry: opts.registry,
   209→      request_timeout: opts.request_timeout,
   210→      task_supervisor: opts.task_supervisor,
   211→      # Map of session_id => {pid, monitor_ref}
   212→      sse_handlers: %{},
   213→      active_tasks: %{}
   214→    }
   215→
   216→    Logger.metadata(mcp_transport: :streamable_http, mcp_server: server)
   217→
   218→    Logging.transport_event("starting", %{
   219→      transport: :streamable_http,
   220→      server: server
   221→    })
   222→
   223→    Telemetry.execute(
   224→      Telemetry.event_transport_init(),
   225→      %{system_time: System.system_time()},
   226→      %{transport: :streamable_http, server: server}
   227→    )
   228→
   229→    {:ok, state}
   230→  end
   231→
   232→  @impl GenServer
   233→  def handle_call({:register_sse_handler, session_id, pid}, _from, state) do
   234→    ref = Process.monitor(pid)
   235→
   236→    sse_handlers = Map.put(state.sse_handlers, session_id, {pid, ref})
   237→
   238→    Logging.transport_event("sse_handler_registered", %{
   239→      session_id: session_id,
   240→      handler_pid: inspect(pid)
   241→    })
   242→
   243→    {:reply, :ok, %{state | sse_handlers: sse_handlers}}
   244→  end
   245→
   246→  @impl GenServer
   247→  def handle_call({:handle_message, session_id, message, context}, from, state) when is_map(message) do
   248→    server = state.registry.whereis_server(state.server)
   249→    timeout = state.request_timeout
   250→
   251→    cond do
   252→      Message.is_notification(message) ->
   253→        GenServer.cast(server, {:notification, message, session_id, context})
   254→        {:reply, {:ok, nil}, state}
   255→
   256→      Message.is_response(message) or Message.is_error(message) ->
   257→        GenServer.cast(server, {:response, message, session_id, context})
   258→        {:reply, {:ok, nil}, state}
   259→
   260→      true ->
   261→        task =
   262→          Task.Supervisor.async(state.task_supervisor, fn ->
   263→            forward_request_to_server(server, message, session_id, context, timeout)
   264→          end)
   265→
   266→        task_info = %{
   267→          type: :handle_message,
   268→          session_id: session_id,
   269→          from: from
   270→        }
   271→
   272→        {:noreply, put_in(state.active_tasks[task.ref], task_info)}
   273→    end
   274→  end
   275→
   276→  @impl GenServer
   277→  def handle_call({:handle_message_for_sse, session_id, message, context}, from, state) when is_map(message) do
   278→    server = state.registry.whereis_server(state.server)
   279→    timeout = state.request_timeout
   280→
   281→    if Message.is_notification(message) do
   282→      GenServer.cast(server, {:notification, message, session_id, context})
   283→      {:reply, {:ok, nil}, state}
   284→    else
   285→      sse_handler? = Map.has_key?(state.sse_handlers, session_id)
   286→
   287→      task =
   288→        Task.Supervisor.async(state.task_supervisor, fn ->
   289→          forward_request_to_server(
   290→            server,
   291→            message,
   292→            session_id,
   293→            context,
   294→            timeout,
   295→            sse_handler?
   296→          )
   297→        end)
   298→
   299→      task_info = %{
   300→        type: :handle_message_for_sse,
   301→        session_id: session_id,
   302→        from: from,
   303→        has_sse_handler: sse_handler?
   304→      }
   305→
   306→      {:noreply, put_in(state.active_tasks[task.ref], task_info)}
   307→    end
   308→  end
   309→
   310→  @impl GenServer
   311→  def handle_call({:get_sse_handler, session_id}, _from, state) do
   312→    case Map.get(state.sse_handlers, session_id) do
   313→      {pid, _ref} -> {:reply, pid, state}
   314→      nil -> {:reply, nil, state}
   315→    end
   316→  end
   317→
   318→  @impl GenServer
   319→  def handle_call({:route_to_session, session_id, message}, _from, state) do
   320→    case Map.get(state.sse_handlers, session_id) do
   321→      {pid, _ref} ->
   322→        send(pid, {:sse_message, message})
   323→        {:reply, :ok, state}
   324→
   325→      nil ->
   326→        {:reply, {:error, :no_sse_handler}, state}
   327→    end
   328→  end
   329→
   330→  @impl GenServer
   331→  def handle_call({:send_message, message}, _from, state) do
   332→    Logging.transport_event("broadcast_notification", %{
   333→      message_size: byte_size(message),
   334→      active_handlers: map_size(state.sse_handlers)
   335→    })
   336→
   337→    for {_session_id, {pid, _ref}} <- state.sse_handlers do
   338→      send(pid, {:sse_message, message})
   339→    end
   340→
   341→    {:reply, :ok, state}
   342→  end
   343→
   344→  defp forward_request_to_server(server, message, session_id, context, timeout, has_sse_handler \\ false) do
   345→    msg = {:request, message, session_id, context}
   346→
   347→    case GenServer.call(server, msg, timeout) do
   348→      {:ok, response} when has_sse_handler ->
   349→        {:sse, response}
   350→
   351→      {:ok, response} ->
   352→        {:ok, response}
   353→
   354→      {:error, reason} ->
   355→        Logging.transport_event(
   356→          "server_error",
   357→          %{reason: reason, session_id: session_id},
   358→          level: :error
   359→        )
   360→
   361→        {:error, reason}
   362→    end
   363→  catch
   364→    :exit, reason ->
   365→      Logging.transport_event("server_call_failed", %{reason: reason}, level: :error)
   366→      {:error, :server_unavailable}
   367→  end
   368→
   369→  @impl GenServer
   370→  def handle_cast({:unregister_sse_handler, session_id}, state) do
   371→    sse_handlers =
   372→      case Map.get(state.sse_handlers, session_id) do
   373→        {_pid, ref} ->
   374→          Process.demonitor(ref, [:flush])
   375→          Map.delete(state.sse_handlers, session_id)
   376→
   377→        nil ->
   378→          state.sse_handlers
   379→      end
   380→
   381→    {:noreply, %{state | sse_handlers: sse_handlers}}
   382→  end
   383→
   384→  @impl GenServer
   385→  def handle_cast(:shutdown, state) do
   386→    Logging.transport_event("shutdown", %{transport: :streamable_http}, level: :info)
   387→
   388→    for {_session_id, {pid, _ref}} <- state.sse_handlers do
   389→      send(pid, :close_sse)
   390→    end
   391→
   392→    Telemetry.execute(
   393→      Telemetry.event_transport_disconnect(),
   394→      %{system_time: System.system_time()},
   395→      %{transport: :streamable_http, reason: :shutdown}
   396→    )
   397→
   398→    {:stop, :normal, state}
   399→  end
   400→
   401→  # Handle successful task completion
   402→  @impl GenServer
   403→  def handle_info({ref, result}, %{active_tasks: active_tasks} = state)
   404→      when is_reference(ref) and is_map_key(active_tasks, ref) do
   405→    {task_info, active_tasks} = Map.pop(active_tasks, ref)
   406→    GenServer.reply(task_info.from, result)
   407→    Process.demonitor(ref, [:flush])
   408→
   409→    {:noreply, %{state | active_tasks: active_tasks}}
   410→  end
   411→
   412→  def handle_info({_ref, _}, state), do: {:noreply, state}
   413→
   414→  def handle_info({:DOWN, ref, :process, _pid, reason}, %{active_tasks: active_tasks} = state)
   415→      when is_map_key(active_tasks, ref) do
   416→    {task_info, active_tasks} = Map.pop(active_tasks, ref)
   417→    error = {:error, {:task_crashed, reason}}
   418→    GenServer.reply(task_info.from, error)
   419→
   420→    Logging.transport_event(
   421→      "task_crashed",
   422→      %{
   423→        reason: inspect(reason, pretty: true),
   424→        session_id: task_info.session_id
   425→      },
   426→      level: :error
   427→    )
   428→
   429→    {:noreply, %{state | active_tasks: active_tasks}}
   430→  end
   431→
   432→  def handle_info({:DOWN, ref, :process, pid, reason}, state) do
   433→    sse_handlers =
   434→      state.sse_handlers
   435→      |> Enum.reject(fn {_session_id, {handler_pid, monitor_ref}} ->
   436→        handler_pid == pid and monitor_ref == ref
   437→      end)
   438→      |> Map.new()
   439→
   440→    if map_size(sse_handlers) < map_size(state.sse_handlers) do
   441→      Logging.transport_event("sse_handler_down", %{reason: inspect(reason)})
   442→    end
   443→
   444→    {:noreply, %{state | sse_handlers: sse_handlers}}
   445→  end
   446→
   447→  def handle_info(_msg, state) do
   448→    {:noreply, state}
   449→  end
   450→
   451→  @impl GenServer
   452→  def terminate(reason, _state) do
   453→    Telemetry.execute(
   454→      Telemetry.event_transport_terminate(),
   455→      %{system_time: System.system_time()},
   456→      %{transport: :streamable_http, reason: reason}
   457→    )
   458→
   459→    :ok
   460→  end
   461→end

defmodule Hermes.Server.Transport.StreamableHTTP do
  @moduledoc """
  StreamableHTTP transport implementation for MCP servers.

  This module provides an HTTP-based transport layer that supports multiple
  concurrent client sessions through Server-Sent Events (SSE). It enables
  web-based MCP clients to communicate with the server using standard HTTP
  protocols.

  ## Features

  - Multiple concurrent client sessions
  - Server-Sent Events for real-time server-to-client communication
  - HTTP POST endpoint for client-to-server messages
  - Automatic session cleanup on disconnect
  - Integration with Phoenix/Plug applications

  ## Usage

  StreamableHTTP is typically started through the server supervisor:

      Hermes.Server.start_link(MyServer, [],
        transport: :streamable_http,
        streamable_http: [port: 4000]
      )

  For integration with existing Phoenix/Plug applications:

      # In your router
      forward "/mcp", Hermes.Server.Transport.StreamableHTTP.Plug,
        server: MyApp.MCPServer

  ## Message Flow

  1. Client connects to `/sse` endpoint, receives a session ID
  2. Client sends messages via POST to `/messages` with session ID header
  3. Server responses are pushed through the SSE connection
  4. Connection closes on client disconnect or server shutdown

  ## Configuration

  - `:port` - HTTP server port (default: 4000)
  - `:server` - The MCP server process to connect to
  - `:name` - Process registration name
  """

  @behaviour Hermes.Transport.Behaviour

  use GenServer
  use Hermes.Logging

  import Peri

  alias Hermes.MCP.Message
  alias Hermes.Telemetry
  alias Hermes.Transport.Behaviour, as: Transport

  require Message

  @type t :: GenServer.server()

  @typedoc """
  StreamableHTTP transport options

  - `:server` - The server process (required)
  - `:name` - Name for registering the GenServer (required)
  """
  @type option ::
          {:server, GenServer.server()}
          | {:name, GenServer.name()}
          | GenServer.option()

  defschema(:parse_options, [
    {:server, {:required, Hermes.get_schema(:process_name)}},
    {:name, {:required, {:custom, &Hermes.genserver_name/1}}},
    {:registry, {:atom, {:default, Hermes.Server.Registry}}},
    {:request_timeout, {:integer, {:default, to_timeout(second: 30)}}},
    {:task_supervisor, {:required, {:custom, &Hermes.genserver_name/1}}}
  ])

  @doc """
  Starts the StreamableHTTP transport.
  """
  @impl Transport
  @spec start_link(Enumerable.t(option())) :: GenServer.on_start()
  def start_link(opts) do
    opts = parse_options!(opts)
    {name, opts} = Keyword.pop!(opts, :name)

    GenServer.start_link(__MODULE__, Map.new(opts), name: name)
  end

  @doc """
  Sends a message to the client via the active SSE connection.

  This function is used for server-initiated notifications.
  It will broadcast to all active SSE connections.

  ## Parameters
    * `transport` - The transport process
    * `message` - The message to send

  ## Returns
    * `:ok` if message was sent successfully
    * `{:error, reason}` otherwise
  """
  @impl Transport
  @spec send_message(GenServer.server(), binary(), keyword()) :: :ok | {:error, term()}
  def send_message(transport, message, _opts \\ []) when is_binary(message) do
    GenServer.call(transport, {:send_message, message})
  end

  @doc """
  Shuts down the transport connection.

  This terminates all active sessions managed by this transport.

  ## Parameters
    * `transport` - The transport process
  """
  @impl Transport
  @spec shutdown(GenServer.server()) :: :ok
  def shutdown(transport) do
    GenServer.cast(transport, :shutdown)
  end

  @impl Transport
  def supported_protocol_versions, do: ["2025-03-26", "2025-06-18"]

  @doc """
  Registers an SSE handler process for a session.

  Called by the Plug when establishing an SSE connection.
  The calling process becomes the SSE handler for the session.
  """
  @spec register_sse_handler(GenServer.server(), String.t()) ::
          :ok | {:error, term()}
  def register_sse_handler(transport, session_id) do
    GenServer.call(transport, {:register_sse_handler, session_id, self()})
  end

  @doc """
  Unregisters an SSE handler process for a session.

  Called when the SSE connection is closed.
  """
  @spec unregister_sse_handler(GenServer.server(), String.t()) :: :ok
  def unregister_sse_handler(transport, session_id) do
    GenServer.cast(transport, {:unregister_sse_handler, session_id})
  end

  @doc """
  Handles an incoming message from a client with request context.

  Called by the Plug when a message is received via HTTP POST.
  """
  @spec handle_message(GenServer.server(), String.t(), map() | list(map), map()) ::
          {:ok, binary() | nil} | {:error, term()}
  def handle_message(transport, session_id, message, context) do
    GenServer.call(transport, {:handle_message, session_id, message, context})
  end

  @doc """
  Handles an incoming message with context and returns {:sse, response} if SSE handler exists.

  This allows the Plug to know whether to stream the response via SSE
  or return it as a regular HTTP response.
  """
  @spec handle_message_for_sse(GenServer.server(), String.t(), map(), map()) ::
          {:ok, binary()} | {:sse, binary()} | {:error, term()}
  def handle_message_for_sse(transport, session_id, message, context) do
    GenServer.call(
      transport,
      {:handle_message_for_sse, session_id, message, context}
    )
  end

  @doc """
  Gets the SSE handler process for a session.

  Returns the pid of the process handling SSE for this session,
  or nil if no SSE connection exists.
  """
  @spec get_sse_handler(GenServer.server(), String.t()) :: pid() | nil
  def get_sse_handler(transport, session_id) do
    GenServer.call(transport, {:get_sse_handler, session_id})
  end

  @doc """
  Routes a message to a specific session's SSE handler.

  Used for targeted server notifications to specific clients.
  """
  @spec route_to_session(GenServer.server(), String.t(), binary()) ::
          :ok | {:error, term()}
  def route_to_session(transport, session_id, message) do
    GenServer.call(transport, {:route_to_session, session_id, message})
  end

  # GenServer implementation

  @impl GenServer
  def init(%{server: server} = opts) do
    Process.flag(:trap_exit, true)

    state = %{
      server: server,
      registry: opts.registry,
      request_timeout: opts.request_timeout,
      task_supervisor: opts.task_supervisor,
      # Map of session_id => {pid, monitor_ref}
      sse_handlers: %{},
      active_tasks: %{}
    }

    Logger.metadata(mcp_transport: :streamable_http, mcp_server: server)

    Logging.transport_event("starting", %{
      transport: :streamable_http,
      server: server
    })

    Telemetry.execute(
      Telemetry.event_transport_init(),
      %{system_time: System.system_time()},
      %{transport: :streamable_http, server: server}
    )

    {:ok, state}
  end

  @impl GenServer
  def handle_call({:register_sse_handler, session_id, pid}, _from, state) do
    ref = Process.monitor(pid)

    sse_handlers = Map.put(state.sse_handlers, session_id, {pid, ref})

    Logging.transport_event("sse_handler_registered", %{
      session_id: session_id,
      handler_pid: inspect(pid)
    })

    {:reply, :ok, %{state | sse_handlers: sse_handlers}}
  end

  @impl GenServer
  def handle_call({:handle_message, session_id, message, context}, from, state) when is_map(message) do
    server = state.registry.whereis_server(state.server)
    timeout = state.request_timeout

    cond do
      Message.is_notification(message) ->
        GenServer.cast(server, {:notification, message, session_id, context})
        {:reply, {:ok, nil}, state}

      Message.is_response(message) or Message.is_error(message) ->
        GenServer.cast(server, {:response, message, session_id, context})
        {:reply, {:ok, nil}, state}

      true ->
        task =
          Task.Supervisor.async(state.task_supervisor, fn ->
            forward_request_to_server(server, message, session_id, context, timeout)
          end)

        task_info = %{
          type: :handle_message,
          session_id: session_id,
          from: from
        }

        {:noreply, put_in(state.active_tasks[task.ref], task_info)}
    end
  end

  @impl GenServer
  def handle_call({:handle_message_for_sse, session_id, message, context}, from, state) when is_map(message) do
    server = state.registry.whereis_server(state.server)
    timeout = state.request_timeout

    if Message.is_notification(message) do
      GenServer.cast(server, {:notification, message, session_id, context})
      {:reply, {:ok, nil}, state}
    else
      sse_handler? = Map.has_key?(state.sse_handlers, session_id)

      task =
        Task.Supervisor.async(state.task_supervisor, fn ->
          forward_request_to_server(
            server,
            message,
            session_id,
            context,
            timeout,
            sse_handler?
          )
        end)

      task_info = %{
        type: :handle_message_for_sse,
        session_id: session_id,
        from: from,
        has_sse_handler: sse_handler?
      }

      {:noreply, put_in(state.active_tasks[task.ref], task_info)}
    end
  end

  @impl GenServer
  def handle_call({:get_sse_handler, session_id}, _from, state) do
    case Map.get(state.sse_handlers, session_id) do
      {pid, _ref} -> {:reply, pid, state}
      nil -> {:reply, nil, state}
    end
  end

  @impl GenServer
  def handle_call({:route_to_session, session_id, message}, _from, state) do
    case Map.get(state.sse_handlers, session_id) do
      {pid, _ref} ->
        send(pid, {:sse_message, message})
        {:reply, :ok, state}

      nil ->
        {:reply, {:error, :no_sse_handler}, state}
    end
  end

  @impl GenServer
  def handle_call({:send_message, message}, _from, state) do
    Logging.transport_event("broadcast_notification", %{
      message_size: byte_size(message),
      active_handlers: map_size(state.sse_handlers)
    })

    for {_session_id, {pid, _ref}} <- state.sse_handlers do
      send(pid, {:sse_message, message})
    end

    {:reply, :ok, state}
  end

  defp forward_request_to_server(server, message, session_id, context, timeout, has_sse_handler \\ false) do
    msg = {:request, message, session_id, context}

    case GenServer.call(server, msg, timeout) do
      {:ok, response} when has_sse_handler ->
        {:sse, response}

      {:ok, response} ->
        {:ok, response}

      {:error, reason} ->
        Logging.transport_event(
          "server_error",
          %{reason: reason, session_id: session_id},
          level: :error
        )

        {:error, reason}
    end
  catch
    :exit, reason ->
      Logging.transport_event("server_call_failed", %{reason: reason}, level: :error)
      {:error, :server_unavailable}
  end

  @impl GenServer
  def handle_cast({:unregister_sse_handler, session_id}, state) do
    sse_handlers =
      case Map.get(state.sse_handlers, session_id) do
        {_pid, ref} ->
          Process.demonitor(ref, [:flush])
          Map.delete(state.sse_handlers, session_id)

        nil ->
          state.sse_handlers
      end

    {:noreply, %{state | sse_handlers: sse_handlers}}
  end

  @impl GenServer
  def handle_cast(:shutdown, state) do
    Logging.transport_event("shutdown", %{transport: :streamable_http}, level: :info)

    for {_session_id, {pid, _ref}} <- state.sse_handlers do
      send(pid, :close_sse)
    end

    Telemetry.execute(
      Telemetry.event_transport_disconnect(),
      %{system_time: System.system_time()},
      %{transport: :streamable_http, reason: :shutdown}
    )

    {:stop, :normal, state}
  end

  # Handle successful task completion
  @impl GenServer
  def handle_info({ref, result}, %{active_tasks: active_tasks} = state)
      when is_reference(ref) and is_map_key(active_tasks, ref) do
    {task_info, active_tasks} = Map.pop(active_tasks, ref)
    GenServer.reply(task_info.from, result)
    Process.demonitor(ref, [:flush])

    {:noreply, %{state | active_tasks: active_tasks}}
  end

  def handle_info({_ref, _}, state), do: {:noreply, state}

  def handle_info({:DOWN, ref, :process, _pid, reason}, %{active_tasks: active_tasks} = state)
      when is_map_key(active_tasks, ref) do
    {task_info, active_tasks} = Map.pop(active_tasks, ref)
    error = {:error, {:task_crashed, reason}}
    GenServer.reply(task_info.from, error)

    Logging.transport_event(
      "task_crashed",
      %{
        reason: inspect(reason, pretty: true),
        session_id: task_info.session_id
      },
      level: :error
    )

    {:noreply, %{state | active_tasks: active_tasks}}
  end

  def handle_info({:DOWN, ref, :process, pid, reason}, state) do
    sse_handlers =
      state.sse_handlers
      |> Enum.reject(fn {_session_id, {handler_pid, monitor_ref}} ->
        handler_pid == pid and monitor_ref == ref
      end)
      |> Map.new()

    if map_size(sse_handlers) < map_size(state.sse_handlers) do
      Logging.transport_event("sse_handler_down", %{reason: inspect(reason)})
    end

    {:noreply, %{state | sse_handlers: sse_handlers}}
  end

  def handle_info(_msg, state) do
    {:noreply, state}
  end

  @impl GenServer
  def terminate(reason, _state) do
    Telemetry.execute(
      Telemetry.event_transport_terminate(),
      %{system_time: System.system_time()},
      %{transport: :streamable_http, reason: reason}
    )

    :ok
  end
end

1→defmodule Tuist.MCP.Server do
     2→  @moduledoc false
     3→
     4→  use Hermes.Server,
     5→    name: "tuist",
     6→    version: "1.4.1",
     7→    capabilities: [
     8→      {:tools, list_changed?: false},
     9→      {:prompts, list_changed?: false}
    10→    ],
    11→    protocol_versions: ["2025-03-26"]
    12→
    13→  alias Hermes.MCP.Error
    14→  alias Hermes.Server.Frame
    15→  alias Hermes.Server.Handlers
    16→  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Prompts.FixFlakyTest
    17→  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.GetTestCase
    18→  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.GetTestCaseRun
    19→  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.GetTestRun
    20→  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListProjects
    21→  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListTestCases
    22→
    23→  component(ListProjects, name: "list_projects")
    24→  component(ListTestCases, name: "list_test_cases")
    25→  component(GetTestCase, name: "get_test_case")
    26→  component(GetTestRun, name: "get_test_run")
    27→  component(GetTestCaseRun, name: "get_test_case_run")
    28→  component(FixFlakyTest, name: "fix_flaky_test")
    29→
    30→  @impl Hermes.Server
    31→  def handle_request(%{"method" => "tools/call", "params" => %{"name" => _name} = params} = request, %Frame{} = frame) do
    32→    request = put_in(request, ["params"], Map.put_new(params, "arguments", %{}))
    33→    Handlers.handle(request, __MODULE__, frame)
    34→  end
    35→
    36→  @impl Hermes.Server
    37→  def handle_request(%{"method" => "tools/call"}, %Frame{} = frame) do
    38→    {:error, invalid_params_error("Missing required parameter: name."), frame}
    39→  end
    40→
    41→  @impl Hermes.Server
    42→  def handle_request(%{"method" => "prompts/get", "params" => %{"name" => _name} = params} = request, %Frame{} = frame) do
    43→    request = put_in(request, ["params"], Map.put_new(params, "arguments", %{}))
    44→    Handlers.handle(request, __MODULE__, frame)
    45→  end
    46→
    47→  @impl Hermes.Server
    48→  def handle_request(%{"method" => "prompts/get"}, %Frame{} = frame) do
    49→    {:error, invalid_params_error("Missing required parameter: name."), frame}
    50→  end
    51→
    52→  @impl Hermes.Server
    53→  def handle_request(request, %Frame{} = frame), do: Handlers.handle(request, __MODULE__, frame)
    54→
    55→  defp invalid_params_error(message) do
    56→    Error.protocol(:invalid_params, %{message: message})
    57→  end
    58→end

defmodule Tuist.MCP.Server do
  @moduledoc false

  use Hermes.Server,
    name: "tuist",
    version: "1.4.1",
    capabilities: [
      {:tools, list_changed?: false},
      {:prompts, list_changed?: false}
    ],
    protocol_versions: ["2025-03-26"]

  alias Hermes.MCP.Error
  alias Hermes.Server.Frame
  alias Hermes.Server.Handlers
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Prompts.FixFlakyTest
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.GetTestCase
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.GetTestCaseRun
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.GetTestRun
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListProjects
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListTestCases

  component(ListProjects, name: "list_projects")
  component(ListTestCases, name: "list_test_cases")
  component(GetTestCase, name: "get_test_case")
  component(GetTestRun, name: "get_test_run")
  component(GetTestCaseRun, name: "get_test_case_run")
  component(FixFlakyTest, name: "fix_flaky_test")

  @impl Hermes.Server
  def handle_request(%{"method" => "tools/call", "params" => %{"name" => _name} = params} = request, %Frame{} = frame) do
    request = put_in(request, ["params"], Map.put_new(params, "arguments", %{}))
    Handlers.handle(request, __MODULE__, frame)
  end

  @impl Hermes.Server
  def handle_request(%{"method" => "tools/call"}, %Frame{} = frame) do
    {:error, invalid_params_error("Missing required parameter: name."), frame}
  end

  @impl Hermes.Server
  def handle_request(%{"method" => "prompts/get", "params" => %{"name" => _name} = params} = request, %Frame{} = frame) do
    request = put_in(request, ["params"], Map.put_new(params, "arguments", %{}))
    Handlers.handle(request, __MODULE__, frame)
  end

  @impl Hermes.Server
  def handle_request(%{"method" => "prompts/get"}, %Frame{} = frame) do
    {:error, invalid_params_error("Missing required parameter: name."), frame}
  end

  @impl Hermes.Server
  def handle_request(request, %Frame{} = frame), do: Handlers.handle(request, __MODULE__, frame)

  defp invalid_params_error(message) do
    Error.protocol(:invalid_params, %{message: message})
  end
end

if Code.ensure_loaded?(Plug) do
  defmodule Hermes.SSE.Streaming do
    @moduledoc false

    use Hermes.Logging

    alias Hermes.SSE.Event

    @type conn :: Plug.Conn.t()
    @type transport :: GenServer.server()
    @type session_id :: String.t()

    @doc """
    Starts the SSE streaming loop for a connection.

    This function takes control of the connection and enters a receive loop,
    streaming messages to the client as they arrive.

    ## Parameters
      - `conn` - The Plug.Conn that has been prepared for chunked response
      - `transport` - The transport process
      - `session_id` - The session identifier
      - `opts` - Options including:
        - `:initial_event_id` - Starting event ID (default: 0)
        - `:on_close` - Function to call when connection closes

    ## Messages handled
      - `{:sse_message, binary}` - Message to send to client
      - `:close_sse` - Close the connection gracefully
    """
    @spec start(conn, transport, session_id, keyword()) :: conn
    def start(conn, transport, session_id, opts \\ []) do
      initial_event_id = Keyword.get(opts, :initial_event_id, 0)
      on_close = Keyword.get(opts, :on_close, fn -> :ok end)

      try do
        loop(conn, transport, session_id, initial_event_id)
      after
        on_close.()
      end
    end

    @doc """
    Prepares a connection for SSE streaming.

    Sets appropriate headers and starts chunked response.
    """
    @spec prepare_connection(conn) :: conn
    def prepare_connection(conn) do
      conn
      |> Plug.Conn.put_resp_content_type("text/event-stream")
      |> Plug.Conn.put_resp_header("cache-control", "no-cache")
      |> Plug.Conn.put_resp_header("x-accel-buffering", "no")
      |> Plug.Conn.send_chunked(200)
    end

    @doc """
    Sends a single SSE event.

    This is useful for sending events outside of the main loop.
    """
    @spec send_event(conn, binary(), non_neg_integer()) ::
            {:ok, conn} | {:error, term()}
    def send_event(conn, data, event_id) when is_binary(data) do
      event = %Event{
        id: to_string(event_id),
        event: "message",
        data: data
      }

      case Plug.Conn.chunk(conn, Event.encode(event)) do
        {:ok, conn} -> {:ok, conn}
        {:error, reason} -> {:error, reason}
      end
    end

    # Private functions

    defp loop(conn, transport, session_id, event_counter) do
      receive do
        {:sse_message, message} when is_binary(message) ->
          case send_event(conn, message, event_counter) do
            {:ok, conn} ->
              loop(conn, transport, session_id, event_counter + 1)

            {:error, reason} ->
              Logging.transport_event(
                "sse_send_failed",
                %{
                  session_id: session_id,
                  reason: reason
                },
                level: :error
              )

              conn
          end

        :close_sse ->
          Logging.transport_event("sse_closing", %{session_id: session_id})
          Plug.Conn.halt(conn)

        {:plug_conn, :sent} ->
          # Ignore Plug internal messages
          loop(conn, transport, session_id, event_counter)

        msg ->
          Logging.transport_event(
            "sse_unknown_message",
            %{
              session_id: session_id,
              message: inspect(msg)
            },
            level: :warning
          )

          loop(conn, transport, session_id, event_counter)
      end
    end
  end
end

content.text:
Now I understand the flow. Here's the key insight:

When Claude Code's MCP client sends a POST request to `/mcp` with a `tools/call` for `list_projects`, the flow depends on the `Accept` header:

1. If `Accept` includes `text/event-stream`, the plug calls `handle_request_with_possible_sse` (line 215 of plug.ex)
2. This goes to `handle_sse_request` which calls `StreamableHTTP.handle_message_for_sse`
3. If no SSE handler exists for the session, it returns `{:sse, response}` 
4. Then `route_sse_response` is called (line 279), which calls `establish_sse_for_request` (line 312)
5. This starts a background task to process the request AND enters the SSE streaming loop (`start_sse_streaming`)
6. The SSE streaming loop (`Streaming.start`) is a **blocking receive loop** that never exits until the client disconnects or `:close_sse` is sent

The problem: **The SSE streaming loop keeps the HTTP connection open indefinitely**. After the tool response is sent as an SSE event, the connection stays open waiting for more messages. The MCP client (Claude Code) likely waits for the HTTP response to complete, but it never does because the SSE stream stays open.

Let me verify how the `validate_accept_header` works to confirm this is the path being taken.

Looking at the plug code:
- `validate_accept_header` (line 367) requires BOTH `application/json` AND `text/event-stream` in the `Accept` header for POST requests
- `wants_sse?` (line 360) checks if `text/event-stream` is in `Accept`
- Since the validation requires both, and `wants_sse?` checks for `text/event-stream`, POST requests that pass validation will **always** go through the SSE path

This means **every `tools/call` POST request enters the SSE streaming loop**, which is a blocking infinite receive loop. The tool result gets sent as an SSE event, but the connection never closes.

The MCP client in Claude Code is likely expecting a simple JSON response (HTTP 200 with a JSON body) for tool calls, but instead gets an SSE stream that stays open after delivering the result.

Let me check if there's a `request_timeout` or any mechanism that closes the SSE stream after the response.

content.text:
Now I have the full picture. Here's my analysis:

---

**The `list_projects` tool hangs because the Hermes StreamableHTTP Plug enters an SSE streaming loop that never terminates.**

Here's the exact flow:

1. Claude Code's MCP client POSTs a `tools/call` request with `Accept: application/json, text/event-stream` (required by `validate_accept_header` at line 367 of plug.ex)
2. Since the `Accept` header contains `text/event-stream`, `wants_sse?` returns `true` (line 360)
3. The request goes through `handle_sse_request` → `handle_message_for_sse` → `route_sse_response`
4. Since there's no existing SSE handler for the session, `establish_sse_for_request` is called (line 312)
5. This does two things:
   - Starts a **background Task** to process the request and get the tool result
   - Calls `start_sse_streaming()` which enters `Streaming.loop/4` -- a **blocking `receive` loop** (line 79 of streaming.ex)
6. The background task completes, sends `{:sse_message, response}` to the loop, which sends the result as an SSE event chunk
7. **But the loop keeps running**, waiting for more messages that never come
8. The HTTP response never "completes" from the client's perspective, so Claude Code keeps waiting

The connection hangs indefinitely because:
- The SSE loop only exits on `:close_sse` message or a send error
- No timeout mechanism exists in the streaming loop
- The `request_timeout` (30s) only applies to the GenServer call to process the tool request, not to the SSE stream itself

**Possible fixes:**

1. **Close the SSE stream after sending the response** -- For tool call responses where the server initiates the SSE stream inline (not a pre-existing GET-based stream), close the stream after the response is sent. This could be done in `establish_sse_for_request` by sending `:close_sse` after the background task completes.

2. **Add an idle timeout to the SSE loop** -- Add a timeout to the `receive` in `Streaming.loop/4` so the stream closes after a period of inactivity.

3. **Don't use SSE for simple request/response** -- In `handle_request_with_possible_sse`, if there's no pre-existing SSE handler, treat it as a regular JSON request instead of establishing a new SSE stream. This is probably the cleanest fix.

Option 3 would look like changing the logic in `handle_sse_request` so that when `route_sse_response` finds no existing SSE handler, it falls back to `handle_json_request` instead of calling `establish_sse_for_request`. However, this change would need to be in the `hermes_mcp` library itself, or worked around in the Tuist server code.

Would you like me to look into implementing a fix? The most practical approach would likely be upgrading `hermes_mcp` if a newer version addresses this, or implementing a workaround in the Tuist MCP pipeline.

1→# Hermes MCP
     2→
     3→[![hex.pm](https://img.shields.io/hexpm/v/hermes_mcp.svg)](https://hex.pm/packages/hermes_mcp)
     4→[![docs](https://img.shields.io/badge/hex-docs-blue.svg)](https://hexdocs.pm/hermes_mcp)
     5→[![ci](https://github.com/cloudwalk/hermes-mcp/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/cloudwalk/hermes-mcp/actions/workflows/ci.yml)
     6→[![Hex Downloads](https://img.shields.io/hexpm/dt/hermes_mcp)](https://hex.pm/packages/hermes_mcp)
     7→
     8→A high-performance Model Context Protocol (MCP) implementation in Elixir.
     9→
    10→## Overview
    11→
    12→Hermes MCP is a comprehensive Elixir SDK for the [Model Context Protocol](https://spec.modelcontextprotocol.io/), providing complete client and server implementations with Elixir's exceptional concurrency model and fault tolerance.
    13→
    14→## Installation
    15→
    16→```elixir
    17→def deps do
    18→  [
    19→    {:hermes_mcp, "~> 0.14.1"}  # x-release-please-version
    20→  ]
    21→end
    22→```
    23→
    24→## Quick Start
    25→
    26→### Server
    27→
    28→```elixir
    29→# Define a server with tools capabilities
    30→defmodule MyApp.MCPServer do
    31→  use Hermes.Server,
    32→    name: "My Server",
    33→    version: "1.0.0",
    34→    capabilities: [:tools]
    35→
    36→  @impl true
    37→  # this callback will be called when the
    38→  # MCP initialize lifecycle completes
    39→  def init(_client_info, frame) do
    40→    {:ok,frame
    41→      |> assign(counter: 0)
    42→      |> register_tool("echo",
    43→        input_schema: %{
    44→          text: {:required, :string, max: 150, description: "the text to be echoed"}
    45→        },
    46→        annotations: %{read_only: true},
    47→        description: "echoes everything the user says to the LLM") }
    48→  end
    49→
    50→  @impl true
    51→  def handle_tool("echo", %{text: text}, frame) do
    52→    Logger.info("This tool was called #{frame.assigns.counter + 1}")
    53→    {:reply, text, assign(frame, counter: frame.assigns.counter + 1)}
    54→  end
    55→end
    56→
    57→# Add to your application supervisor
    58→children = [
    59→  Hermes.Server.Registry,
    60→  {MyApp.MCPServer, transport: :streamable_http}
    61→]
    62→
    63→# Add to your Plug/Phoenix router (if using HTTP)
    64→forward "/mcp", to: Hermes.Server.Transport.StreamableHTTP.Plug, init_opts: [server: MyApp.MCPServer]
    65→```
    66→
    67→Now you can achieve your MCP server on `http://localhost:<port>/mcp`
    68→
    69→### Client
    70→
    71→```elixir
    72→# Define a client module
    73→defmodule MyApp.MCPClient do
    74→  use Hermes.Client,
    75→    name: "MyApp",
    76→    version: "1.0.0",
    77→    protocol_version: "2025-03-26"
    78→end
    79→
    80→# Add to your application supervisor
    81→children = [
    82→  {MyApp.MCPClient,
    83→   transport: {:streamable_http, base_url: "http://localhost:4000"}}
    84→]
    85→
    86→# Use the client
    87→{:ok, result} = MyApp.MCPClient.call_tool("echo", %{text: "this will be echoed!"})
    88→```
    89→
    90→## Why Hermes?
    91→
    92→Named after Hermes, the Greek god of boundaries and communication, this library facilitates seamless interaction between Large Language Models and external tools - serving as a messenger between AI and data sources.
    93→
    94→## Documentation
    95→
    96→For detailed guides and examples, visit the [official documentation](https://hexdocs.pm/hermes_mcp).
    97→
    98→## Examples
    99→
   100→We have build some elixir implementation examples using `plug` based and `phoenix` apps:
   101→
   102→1. [upcase-server](/priv/dev/upcase/README.md): `plug` based MCP server using streamable_http
   103→2. [echo-elixir](/priv/dev/echo-elixir/README.md): `phoenix` based MCP server using sse
   104→3. [ascii-server](/priv/dev/ascii/README.md): `phoenix_live_view` based MCP server using streamable_http and UI
   105→
   106→## License
   107→
   108→MIT License. See [LICENSE](./LICENSE) for details.
   109→
